news_summary: Amex Exploration announced discovery of a copper-rich VMS zone at its Perron property, reporting 2.40% Cu over 7.8 m (including 3.92% Cu over 4.4 m) and accompanying Zn/Au/Ag grades. It also mentions a follow-up hole at depth, awaiting assays. The article is copper-focused and exploration-stage. | Antaike said China’s 22 surveyed smelters produced 747,200 tonnes of refined copper cathodes in July, roughly flat versus June, with output expected to rise in August. Copper concentrate treatment charges rose to $57.50/t. The report covers tin separately and includes no aluminium or nickel impact. | Japanese beverage vendors are gradually shifting from plastic bottles to aluminum cans to reduce marine plastic pollution. The move suggests incremental demand for aluminium packaging materials and recycling streams as brands replace one-way plastic with can formats, potentially supporting short-term sentiment for aluminium prices.

news_summary: Copper prices rose as supply worries grew in Chile, with mine workers at Caserones walking off and mediation talks extending at BHP’s Escondida to avoid strikes. Separately, power consumption restrictions in China’s Guangxi region could temporarily shut a large share of aluminum smelting capacity, supporting aluminium. | SMM reports aluminium futures at SHFE have risen and are near a 13-year high, with international prices up ~29% YTD. However, it does not announce new assays or production, limiting near-term price impact. | Eagle Plains reports Rockridge will mobilize a summer/fall exploration program at the Knife Lake copper VMS project in Saskatchewan, using VTEM plus geophysics and follow-up drilling. Impact on metals prices is likely limited. | Forum Energy Metals commenced drilling at its Love Lake nickel-copper-PGM project in Saskatchewan. news_summary: SMM reports secondary copper-based rod producer operating rates fell to 59.53% in July, down 7.59 points month-on-month, amid power curtailment, late-July floods, and furnace repairs in Henan. Tight secondary copper supply forced plants to reduce or suspend output, with August rates expected to decline further. | SMM says operating rates of copper billet producers fell to 57.64% in July, down month on month, with backlog from June only partially supporting output. Demand for copper-zinc alloy billets was weak due to off-season conditions and reduced refrigeration orders, while August rates are expected to decline further.

news_summary: SMM reports aluminium billet stocks in major consumption areas rose 14,400 mt to 117,600 mt (+14%) on the week, while outbound shipments fell 4,900 mt to 41,200 mt amid quiet downstream purchasing. Downstream orders are sluggish and SMM expects demand to soften and supply to improve, with possible restocking next week. | SMM data showed China’s social aluminium inventories rose 7,000 mt on the week to 739,000 mt as of Aug 12. Shortage of arrivals eased in Wuxi and outbound volumes declined, while Hainan received more ingot from north-west regions. Rigid downstream demand continued despite high prices. | Fastmarkets/Fastmarkets-Metal Bulletin live futures commentary reports nickel is up 2% and highlights ongoing aluminium market conditions. The aluminium section stresses supply has been technically intact despite some curtailments and logistics disruption, suggesting mixed near-term momentum versus nickel’s clear gains.

news_summary: London copper prices rose 0.4% after strikes at two Chilean mines (Codelco’s Andina and JX Nippon’s Caserones) heightened supply disruption risk. Escondida approved a contract, limiting upside. Fitch cited tightened copper and aluminum markets from supply constraints, while weaker China demand weighed sentiment.

news_summary: Copper prices fell for a third day to the lowest in almost two months, driven by weak economic data reinforcing demand concerns and nervousness ahead of central bank messaging at Jackson Hole. The selloff is echoed by declines in LME aluminum and nickel, reflecting broader risk-off pressure on industrial metals. | The EU proposes a CBAM on certain imports to prevent carbon leakage, phasing in from 2023 and fully implemented from 2026. It would require importers to buy and surrender certificates based on embedded emissions, using EU ETS auction prices. Aluminium is explicitly in scope; carbon-costs may raise import costs and weigh on demand.

news_summary: SMM reports that Chinese aluminium prices fluctuated at high levels. A00 aluminium eased by 60 yuan/mt to 20,540 yuan/mt, while secondary aluminium prices remained firm. Imports of aluminium ingots thinned, and die-casting demand stayed relatively rigid despite high production costs. Secondary aluminium is expected to fluctuate. | SMM data showed China’s social aluminium billet inventories fell 6,200 mt week-on-week to 124,400 mt as of Aug 26, led by declines in Foshan, Wuxi and Nanchang. Downstream restocking reflected high aluminium prices, but billet demand weakened; inventories are expected to stay muted next week due to low arrivals. | Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) is considering an IPO for H1 2022 and is weighing valuations and investor appetite. Sources say recent record aluminum prices in Europe and the US could influence the decision. EGA has smelters in the UAE and a bauxite mine in Guinea, valued by banks at $16–19B. | Reuters reports that Changji prefecture in China’s Xinjiang ordered several aluminium smelters to limit production from August, cutting monthly output by about 10% versus Jan–Jul levels. Xinjiang is a key global supply hub, so the supply restriction is supportive for short-term aluminium pricing.

The jump is linked to disruptions to global supply, including COVID-related slowdowns, elevated transportation costs, U.S. import restrictions, and labor shortages, tightening availability and boosting prices. | Aluminum prices are reported to have surged 47% this year, reaching $2,907/tonne on the LME three-month offer, raising construction material costs and project pricing. The article links higher aluminum (and other inputs like copper) to increased builder expenses and reduced confidence, but does not suggest near-term relief. | Aluminum prices rose more than 10% in September as analysts point to tight electrolytic aluminum supply under power/"double control" limits and stricter autumn-winter production curbs. Political turmoil in Guinea raised bauxite supply concerns. Spot inventories fell ahead of the “Golden Nine Silver Ten” demand peak, supporting higher prices.

news_summary: Copper prices rose after China’s central bank injected 100 billion yuan via reverse repos to ease market nerves from Evergrande’s debt woes. Three-month LME copper climbed and ShFE inventories fell to the lowest since August 2009, supporting tighter supply expectations. Other base metals mixed, reflecting risk sentiment effects. | Copper prices rose after China’s central bank injected 100 billion yuan via reverse repo to calm markets amid Evergrande debt stress. Three-month LME copper increased while ShFE contracts also saw mixed moves. Copper inventories on ShFE fell to the lowest since Aug 2009, adding support. Nickel and aluminium moved with the broader complex. | Shanghai nonferrous metals closed mixed as power rationing disrupted production. Copper rose amid a macro-driven stronger USD and cautious positioning, while aluminium fell on renewed rumours of power cuts affecting upstream aluminium/alumina plants. Nickel plunged on weaker stainless-steel demand and possible Indonesia nickel-export limits, despite firm ore and ferronickel. | Copper rose after China’s central bank injected 100 billion yuan via reverse repos to calm financial markets amid Evergrande debt worries. The liquidity easing boosted sentiment for China-demand metals, lifting LME copper. The same risk-off/risk-on shift coincided with mixed but generally supportive moves across other base metals. | Guinea’s coup raises fears of disruptions to bauxite exports, a key aluminium feedstock. Rusal may recall staff, and China-dependent supply appears constrained. The piece also cites aluminium futures rising toward ~$2,900/tonne amid declining Chinese output, implying upward near-term pressure for aluminium prices. | Ghana’s GIADEC selected Rocksure International to develop a $1.2bn integrated bauxite mine and refinery in Ashanti, aiming to convert bauxite into aluminium products. The project could eventually produce substantial throughput, but construction timing suggests little immediate effect on near-term aluminium market balances. | Live futures commentary reports lead down over the week and states aluminium has returned above $2,900. It also discusses conditions affecting aluminium availability—supply curtailments in parts of the Gulf, inventory buffer drawdowns/repositioning, and severe shipping disruption through the Strait of Hormuz.

The PBOC also injected liquidity as expiring loans rolled over, supporting risk sentiment. The deal suggests incremental demand for extruded and rolled aluminium components. | Aluminium is near a 13-year high as China reins in production to meet emissions rules, facing tight power supplies and higher electricity/coal costs. The piece frames the move as a market reaction to macro-economic uncertainty, suggesting investors are scaling back expectations for near-term demand and price momentum in aluminium. | Aluminium jumped to a 13-year high on the LME as deepening energy stress raised smelting costs and squeezed output. news_summary: SMM reports aluminium prices stayed firm after Guinea’s early-September coup raised concerns about bauxite supply. Tight power from energy-consumption controls and higher coal prices constrained downstream production, while reported cuts/shutdowns in China and Guinea supported tight global supply expectations. SHFE aluminium surged to records and LME hit new highs. | The article says China’s secondary aluminium output has grown to about 14 million mt (around 30% of global) and relies mainly on domestic scrap. Such a warning points to increasing aluminium input prices and near-term cost pressure, which is typically supportive for aluminium price expectations over the coming weeks.

Planned additional supply and a continuing supply gap outlook influence near-term aluminium sentiment. | Rusal plans to supply 300,000–400,000 mt of aluminium to China in 2021, similar to 2020, and plans significantly higher sales next year. news_summary: SMM says alumina prices in China rose to about 4,073 yuan/mt as of Oct 15, aided by tight overseas supply. Flood-related outages reduced output at Shanxi refineries, while high bauxite, coal, and caustic soda costs constrained purchases. Spot inventories are low, keeping alumina prices elevated this week. | Canada’s primary aluminium output fell to 244,607 tonnes in September, down 4.7% from a year earlier, according to Alcan Association of Canada data. news_summary: A live futures update states that LME aluminium fell 3.3% on high trading volume. The article also mentions Fastmarkets/Metal Bulletin database corrections and procedural delays for certain aluminium differential and billet premium assessments. Overall, the immediate market signal is negative for aluminium prices, while no clear copper or nickel impact is indicated. | A Reuters poll expects copper to keep falling as China’s growth slows and mine supply ramps up, shifting the market to a surplus. Aluminium price gains are expected to ease as the deficit shrinks. Nickel is also seen sliding as Indonesia’s supply increases and the market turns to a surplus in 2022. | The piece argues that improving airline/aviation activity will lift aluminium demand. Airbus plans to ramp A320 build rates sharply (above pre-pandemic levels), and leasing firms question whether aircraft deliveries rise as fast as expected. The supply chain, including aluminium rolling mills, could face shortages, supporting aluminium prices. | New Zealand’s Electricity Authority says households pay about $200 more per year partly due to Meridian and Contact deals supplying the Tiwai aluminium smelter with cheaper power. Business NZ says rising power prices also reflect low water conditions and limited gas and wind generation, not only the smelter.

news_summary: Shanghai aluminum hit a three-month low as thermal coal prices tumbled, easing concerns about rising production costs for the energy-intensive metal. The piece also notes sharp selloffs in Shanghai copper and nickel alongside broader LME/LME contract moves, attributing the shift largely to coal policy and energy-cost relief. | SMM says aluminium billet stocks in five key consumption areas fell 18,100 mt to 163,100 mt (-9.97%) week on week. Drops in Foshan and Nanchang drove most of the decline, while power rationing-related demand weakness weighed on Wuxi. Less arrivals and reduced output suggest inventories may fall again next week.

news_summary: Novelis will invest $375 million to expand its Zhenjiang aluminium recycling and production site in China for automotive sheet products, aiming for completion by mid-2024. The company processes over two million tonnes of scrap annually and says recycling uses far less energy, potentially supporting aluminium supply and demand sentiment in the near term. | Montana has stopped making license plates because of disruptions in U.S. aluminum supply, presented as an ongoing supply-chain issue. The halt indicates limited availability of aluminum for fabrication demand, which can increase short-term concerns about aluminum tightness and support near-term pricing expectations.

news_summary: The U.S. Department of Commerce said it is consulting with Japan on steel and aluminium trade issues, especially the effect of global overcapacity on aluminium markets. The statement highlights restoring industry market positioning and addressing carbon emissions, but offers no concrete trade measures, leaving near-term aluminium price impact uncertain. | SMM reports aluminium prices fell sharply (about 25% by Oct 29) amid weaker energy-cost support as coal prices continued to drop. Bad-weather arrivals increased aluminium inventories in China to 100,300 mt. Power rationing eased and downstream restocking improved, so panic eased, but concentrated arrivals cap upside, implying only a limited rebound. | The piece argues that behind aluminium’s price moves, primary production costs have risen sharply, compressing producers’ margins. This “secular shift to higher costs” implies tighter profitability and potential supply strain, which could support higher aluminium prices over the coming days to weeks. | The U.S. and EU agreed to ease trade tensions: the U.S. will lift tariffs on some EU steel and aluminum imports starting Dec. 1 via a duty-free quota, while the EU removes retaliatory tariffs on U.S. goods. The partial tariff relief should support aluminum supply and prices near term.

news_summary: Biden and the EU announced a pause and revised approach to US-EU Section 232 tariffs on steel and aluminum. The deal uses tariff-rate quotas allowing most EU steel duty-free up to quotas, removes retaliation, and ends WTO disputes, aiming to address excess capacity and carbon policies. | Amcor temporarily reduced shipments to some customers after shortages in specialist resins, PET plastic and aluminium constrained production in the September quarter. The company expects conditions to improve by the second half of its 2021-22 financial year, implying continued aluminium tightness in the near term. | Copper prices edged up as low LME exchange inventories provided support after steep recent declines. LME three-month copper rose 0.8% and Shanghai’s December contract climbed 0.6% after two weeks of declines. The planned lifting of Peru’s Antamina blockade further supported sentiment, alongside generally firmer aluminium prices.

news_summary: Alcoa plans to restart long-idled capacity at an Australian aluminium smelter. The restart would raise aluminium production/supply expectations in the near term, affecting market tightness. Traders may adjust positions based on the timing and pace of ramp-up, influencing aluminium price sentiment over coming days to weeks. | Alcoa plans to restart aluminum lines in Australia that have been idle since 2009. The reopening signals additional primary aluminum supply returning to the market, which can weigh on near-term expectations for aluminium balance and push prices lower in the coming days. | U.S. Commerce Secretary Gina Raimondo will visit Japan and, with USTR representatives, discuss the possible cancellation of U.S. tariffs on Japanese steel and aluminium. The talks are aimed at strengthening trade and cooperation and restoring supply chains, which could ease aluminium import costs and support aluminium prices in the near term. | Alcoa plans to restart the Portland aluminium smelter after 12 years of inactivity. The restart is expected to bring additional aluminium output back into the market, improving supply expectations and likely pressuring prices in the near term as traders price in reduced tightness. | Aluminium prices are falling as China’s auto sales weaken, signaling softer downstream demand for the metal. The report also says the power crunch is easing, reducing constraints on aluminium production and potentially easing supply pressure. Together, the developments create downward near-term price bias for aluminium. | Copper rose about 1.2% as China’s export growth beat forecasts, LME-registered copper inventories fell sharply, and canceled warrants suggested further stock declines. Sentiment was reinforced by passage of a $1 trillion U.S. infrastructure bill, though traders remain cautious about potential Chinese demand weakness.

news_summary: LME three-month copper fell 0.88% as traders flagged uncertainty over Chinese demand. The hospital says it currently has a two-month supply and is not yet facing a shortage, but is stockpiling ahead of demand.

news_summary: SMM reports aluminium billet stocks in five major consumption areas fell 7,200 mt (down 9.14%) to 71,700 mt, extending seven straight weeks of decline. The drop is attributed to weaker billet arrivals, led by Foshan. North-west transport remains disrupted, so inventories may fall but more slowly, with possible week-to-week reversal. | Fujifilm said it will raise by 0.39 GBP per sqm the surcharge for aluminum offset printing plates from Jan 1, 2022. It attributes the move to unprecedented aluminum price increases plus higher energy, logistics, chemicals, and packaging costs, and adds a UK order charge to offset local logistics expenses. | Copper prices slipped as Omicron fears and slowing China growth reduced demand outlooks. LME copper was down 0.3% on the day and around 4% on the week, despite strong year-to-date performance. China growth and property guidance stayed cautious, while aluminium inventories rose 16% and other base metals moved mixed. | The US Commerce Department is delaying removal of Trump-era tariffs on UK steel and aluminum, citing UK threats to trigger Article 16 of the Northern Ireland protocol. The 25% steel and 10% aluminum tariffs remain, while negotiations with the UK stall, adding uncertainty over future trade terms despite UK denial of protocol linkage. | Vietnam’s customs holds about 1.8 million tonnes of seized aluminium worth roughly $5bn, but it’s unlikely to be released soon amid an ongoing investigation. With China curbing supply and global inventories drawn down rapidly, the hoard’s inaccessibility reinforces a tight aluminium market and supports higher prices.

news_summary: Montana craft brewers report severe shortages and much higher prices for aluminum beverage cans. Supply-chain clogs, increased can-maker minimum order requirements, and longer lead times force breweries to use intermediaries, raising can costs. The story cites aluminum rising to a decade high and says beer price increases may follow for customers soon. | The piece notes that nickel is edging above $20,000/t, implying improving near-term sentiment for nickel. It also references aluminium supply staying intact despite output curtailments and mentions severe shipping disruption through the Strait of Hormuz, factors that can affect aluminium transport and pricing.

news_summary: Supply chain problems have caused an aluminum can shortage, and Ball Corp plans to raise minimum order sizes, forcing distributors to revise order quantities. Arizona craft breweries report difficulty obtaining normal can volumes, predicting higher costs, price increases, and scaled-back releases into spring, supporting higher aluminum-related prices. | A report says aluminum can demand is high, but shortages of cans available for shipment are affecting Central Alabama breweries. Ball Corporation links the issue to recycling input quality and supply chain constraints, forcing larger orders and higher costs that may be passed to consumers, potentially supporting near-term aluminum sentiment. | The article cites initial producer offers for Q1 MJP aluminium at $195/t and discusses Fastmarkets’ corrections/delays to European low-carbon aluminium differential and 6063 extrusion billet premium assessments. While it is directly linked to aluminium price benchmarks, it does not state a clear increase or decrease versus earlier offers.

news_summary: China’s aluminium exports reached record levels in November, totaling 509,300 mt of unwrought aluminium and aluminium semis (+6.2% month-on-month, +20.1% year-on-year). Total exports in January–November rose 14.9% to 5.06 million mt, per customs data, suggesting increased outward supply. | The UK Trade Secretary says she will urge the U.S. to remove Trump-era tariffs on British steel and aluminum. The move follows an October U.S.-EU deal to drop their levies, which would likely lower aluminum trade barriers and improve supply conditions, supporting near-term aluminum sentiment. | China exported 509,300 mt of unwrought aluminium and aluminium semis in November, a record high since March 2020, with exports up 6.2% month-on-month and 20.1% year-on-year. Rumours of reduced export rebates and strong overseas demand boosted shipments as export profits rose and SHFE/LME ratios stayed low. | Trade experts and the World Bank forecast higher aluminium prices, citing strong demand and ongoing supply concerns. Aluminium output was affected this year by power crises in China, India and other locations, while alumina (needed for refining) output dynamics are highlighted. Prices are expected to rise before easing as energy constraints fade. | U.S. Trade Representative Katherine Tai met the UK trade minister and reiterated U.S. focus on tackling non-market excess capacity in steel and aluminum, including cooperation with partners on trade and carbon intensity. The U.S. also maintains Section 232 tariffs—25% on steel and 10% on aluminum—supportive for near-term aluminum market sentiment.

news_summary: SMM reports aluminium billet inventories in five major consumption areas rose 13,800 mt to 85,500 mt versus the prior week, reversing a seven-week decline. The build was driven by higher billet arrivals and relatively weak downstream demand, and inventories are expected to stay on an upward trajectory. | South Korea’s Fair Trade Commission fined eight aluminium alloy product makers a combined KRW20.7bn for colluding to fix quotation and bidding prices and divide order volumes for carmakers including Hyundai and Kia from 2011-2021. The FTC expects a revised bidding system from next year to prevent future price fixing. | Rockridge completed a helicopter-borne VTEM EM and magnetic survey at its Knife Lake copper project in Saskatchewan and reports encouraging identification of conductors, while field analytical results are still pending. The update supports future drilling targeting for a Cu-Zn-Co VMS deposit, with no immediate production or supply change indicated. | Copper prices fell as Omicron-related concerns eased and the dollar firmed, adding pressure despite still-low LME inventories. LME on-warrant stocks rose to a one-month high, signaling easing nearby tightness. Nickel declined as well, while aluminium rose slightly, showing mixed pressure across industrial metals. | Fathom Nickel announced updated 2021 soil geochemistry at its Albert Lake property near the historic Rottenstone mine, identifying extensive Ni-Cu-Co anomalies consistent with magmatic nickel signatures and planning winter drilling. Nickel and copper exploration potential is increased, but no immediate production changes are stated, limiting near-term price impact.

news_summary: SMM expects China’s waste aluminium supply to become relatively surplus as recycled-aluminium consumption slows with weak real estate and auto end-demand. Import volumes of unwrought aluminium alloys are forecast to fall further as overseas supply/imports tighten. Higher waste treatment costs may support some grades, but overall pressure remains negative. | Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) signed a three-year bauxite supply deal with China’s Bosai Group, providing several million tonnes annually from Guinea, with the first shipment expected in January 2022. It also cites rising Chinese bauxite imports and alumina capacity additions, supporting feedstock availability for aluminium production. | SMM reports aluminium billet conversion margins have stayed high but inventories across China have been declining for seven weeks amid sluggish downstream purchases and falling shipments. The inventory is expected to drop more slowly and possibly turn to accumulation in 1–2 weeks, with conversion margins peaking. Downstream extrusion operating rates may also fall. | Fastmarkets announced a consultation outcome affecting the publication times/holiday schedule of some base metal assessments in Asia, plus delays and corrections to European low-carbon aluminium differential assessments and aluminium 6063 extrusion billet premium midpoints. It also discusses methodology compliance for steelmaking raw-material indices, without citing new aluminium fundamentals. | SMM expects aluminium scrap supply to turn surplus as recycling output rises while downstream consumption in real estate and automobiles stays weak. China’s scrap import limits and logistics frictions reduce unwrought alloy imports. Cost and silicon-price effects may tighten some scrap spreads, but overall scrap surplus implies downward pressure on aluminium-linked pricing. | Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) will supply several million tonnes of Guinean bauxite ore per year to China’s Bosai Minerals Group under a three-year agreement. EGA already delivered over a million tonnes this year. Bauxite is the key input to alumina and aluminium, supporting feedstock reliability for Chinese output. | The article includes a Dow Jones news item noting that a military coup in Guinea has sent aluminum futures to a 10-year high, with Alcoa shares up about 2.3% in premarket. Because Guinea is central to bauxite supply, expectations for aluminum supply tightness should be bullish.

news_summary: SMM cites China Business Industrial Research Institute data showing China imported 2.572 million tons of unwrought aluminium and aluminium products from January to October 2021, up 14.4% year-on-year. Import value rose to $7.518 billion, up 46%, indicating stronger supply inflows that may weigh on aluminium prices. | SMM reports China’s domestic aluminium social inventory fell to 91,500 mt, supporting a brief upside in SHFE trading. However, costs are shrinking as alumina and electricity prices fall. With heating-season production restrictions and colder weather likely weakening demand, SMM expects SHFE aluminium to remain rangebound with slight declines into month-end. | The headline indicates the U.S. is pitching a truce to Japan regarding Trump-era steel and aluminum tariffs. If negotiations lead to reduced or avoided tariff pressure, it would improve expectations for aluminum trade flows and pricing near term, likely providing support to aluminum markets over the coming days.

news_summary: SMM says China’s aluminium scrap supply tightened further, pushing scrap prices up ~200 yuan/mt as recycling fell and processors reduced output after annual targets. Die-casting plants resumed buying and may stockpile ahead of end-month. Secondary aluminium alloy prices stabilised but edged higher despite weak demand and closed import windows. | SMM reports China’s electrolytic aluminium inventory fell to 915,000 tons (-75,000). Spot prices in Guangdong slipped slightly but aluminum discounts narrowed. The piece argues macro support for stable growth and faster inventory movement “into the warehouse” boosts sentiment, while falling alumina prices temper gains. | Shanghai aluminium futures rose to a more-than-two-week peak, up 1.7%, driven by low inventories and improving demand as China eases energy consumption curbs. Reported lack of imminent large-scale output restarts and potential production cuts in northern China add support, lifting both ShFE and LME earlier. | LME prices are described as mostly lower while the report highlights a near 75,000-tonne inflow of aluminium into Asian warehouses. Such warehouse additions increase readily available supply in the region, which can weaken nearby pricing and contribute to the overall downside move in aluminium. | LME aluminium prices fell after LME-registered warehouse inventories jumped by 67,750 tonnes to 977,400 tonnes. Expectations of solid demand and still-tight Chinese supply limited losses, but cash/3-month premiums narrowed and the market reversed earlier gains. Aluminium has dropped 18% since October highs. | Norsk Hydro will invest about US$230mn to convert a Brazil alumina plant to natural gas. The change is intended to alter the plant’s energy inputs, potentially lowering operating costs and improving production economics. Better near-term alumina affordability can support expectations for aluminium output. | The US is offering Japan a deal to resolve disputes over 2018-era tariffs on steel and aluminum. The proposed arrangement would allow a quota of imports to enter the US free of duties, similar to the US-EU suspension. Japan seeks full abolition, but talks are ongoing.

news_summary: LME three-month aluminium fell 0.96% after LME warehouse inventories jumped by 67,750 tonnes, though declines were capped as demand stayed strong and China supply was expected to remain tight. The article also says copper dropped 0.38% with LME registered stocks rising for a fourth day. | China’s aluminium production in November fell 1.8% year-on-year to 3.1 million tons, with electrolytic operating capacity still constrained in some regions due to power reasons. Several areas reduced energy-intensive output amid electricity pressure, and a tank leakage accident occurred at a plant in Yunnan, keeping supply tight. | SMM says alumina prices have plunged, reversing prior gains, and bauxite prices in Shanxi, Henan, and south-west regions are under pressure or expected to fall further. Weaker alumina profitability, low acceptance of high-priced bauxite, increased north China mining and discounts, and planned procurement cuts support bearish near-term aluminium sentiment. | SMM says aluminium fluoride prices rose in November, driven by largely unchanged supply and restocking by some aluminium smelters for winter. Major firms lifted purchasing quotations, with Chinalco’s price up to 12,500 yuan/mt, improving producer profits and operating rates. Fluorite powder gains and expectations point to further December increases. | London copper was flat as investors awaited a key U.S. Fed decision on timing of tapering and potential earlier rate hikes. The dollar’s strength and possible liquidity tightening were a focus. LME aluminium eased, nickel was steady, and Shanghai contracts were slightly lower, indicating limited net near-term momentum.

news_summary: SMM said aluminium billet inventories in five major consumption areas fell 10,000 mt WoW to 75,500 mt on Dec 16, led by sharp declines in Wuxi and Nanchang. Warehouses reported low arrivals because billet makers were less willing to ship amid low conversion margins. Next week inventory is expected to be largely unchanged.

news_summary: China’s bauxite (and concentrates) imports fell 19% month-on-month to 7.7 million tons in November, and were also down year-on-year. Guinea remained the largest supplier but exports to China declined as well. Reduced bauxite inflows can pressure alumina supply expectations and bearishly affect aluminium. | China’s imports of raw (primary) aluminium rose to 229,400 tons in November, up 64% from October and 309% from the same month last year, per China customs data cited by SMM. Imports from India also surged, suggesting stronger near-term physical demand that could support aluminium prices.

news_summary: Alro Slatina, Romania’s major vertically integrated aluminum producer, is launching a total activity shutdown process due to soaring energy prices. As Romania’s largest energy consumer, the firm faces cost pressure that may curb smelting output in the near term, influencing European aluminum supply expectations. | LME and SHFE aluminium prices rose sharply as traders worried European producers in countries such as Montenegro and Germany could restrict output at year-end. The concern stems from energy shortages and surging electricity prices. With Western Europe’s aluminium capacity and operating rates already relatively low, bearish supply risk supports long positions. | China’s aluminium extrusion exports rose to 88,100 mt in November (+18% MoM, +5% YoY), with EU shipments up. The SHFE/LME ratio fell to 7.07, improving export margins and encouraging traders. Exports are expected to correct slightly in December around Christmas/New Year despite lower LME ratio. | China’s raw aluminium imports rose 64% month-on-month in November to 229,000 tonnes, amid production constraints and tighter energy controls affecting smelters. Market participants expect imports to fall in December because domestic aluminium prices have been declining and import buyers face losses. Alumina imports also dropped on oversupply. | The EU plans to impose additional tariffs on imports of Chinese aluminum foil, citing subsidy concerns. The measure is expected to alter trade flows and effective supply into the European market, potentially tightening availability for aluminum foil and supporting near-term aluminum pricing versus a tariff-free outlook. | The article reports that Europe’s largest aluminum smelter has reduced output following a sharp surge in electricity power prices. Higher energy costs can force curtailments in aluminum production, tightening short-term supply and likely providing upward support to aluminum prices in the near term.

news_summary: LME aluminium surged to a two-month high amid high European electricity prices and sharply lower LME inventories, with large cancellations of warehouse receipts suggesting more metal could leave. Aluminium spot prices rose sharply, reinforcing tight European supply fears. LME copper meanwhile settled lower on the day, limiting net cross-metal bullishness. | LME aluminium jumped to a two-month high as high European electricity prices and sharply lower LME stocks/warehouse receipt cancellations raised expectations of severe shortages. Aluminium’s supply tightness is reinforced by soaring spot prices and estimates of European capacity shutdown. LME copper settled lower during the same recovery session. | South32 and Alcoa plan to invest $175m to restart the Alumar aluminium smelter in Brazil, previously on care and maintenance since 2015. Using 100% renewable power, the project targets production in the June quarter of 2022 and up to 447,000 tpa capacity by March 2023. | Aluminium Bahrain (Alba) said it produced an all-time record 1,561,222 tonnes in 2021, beating its 2020 output and marking more than 20 million safe working hours without any lost-time injuries. The release highlights stronger smelting performance at a major ex-China supplier. | US Customs and Border Protection published guidance and Federal Register proclamations for EU steel and aluminum TRQs under Section 232. The aluminum quota begins processing Jan 3, 2022, with quota allocation rules and renewed exclusions through 2023. Reduced effective tariff burden and managed import volumes may weigh on aluminum prices near term.

news_summary: LME copper futures closed down after Federal Reserve minutes signaled interest rates could rise faster, weighing on risk appetite and liquidity. The stronger dollar reduced demand for dollar assets. Yangshan copper premium fell, suggesting softer Chinese import demand, while aluminium also eased as base metals fell despite tight warehouses. | Japan buyers agreed liquid aluminium shipment premiums of $177/mt for Jan–Mar, down 20% from the prior quarter and below $220/mt in Oct–Dec. The cut reflects weaker Japanese spot premiums and overseas prices, signaling reduced demand pressure. Japan is Asia’s largest aluminium importer, with bids above LME. | Aluminium rebounded toward $3,000/t as LME-registered warehouse stocks dropped sharply, with traders fearing Europe’s and Asia’s energy crunch will force additional smelter cutbacks. Analysts cite energy as ~40% of operating costs and warn significant capacity is at risk, reinforcing a worsening supply deficit. | Kentucky’s legislature introduced a bill ordering the state to recover a $15 million investment (with interest/fees) from a failed aluminum mill deal near Ashland, connected to Braidy Industries/Unity Aluminum. The project’s financing fell short amid allegations of fraud. The bill sets deadlines and limits further time for private funding. | Market participants expect European aluminium scrap and secondary ingot prices to stay robust into 2022 despite a sharp recent decline in DIN226. They cite bullish sentiment from tight raw-material supply, rising automotive order inquiries for early 2022, and continued support from record scrap prices and growing demand for low-CO2 recycled aluminium.

news_summary: Europe’s aluminium smelters face a winter power crunch, with operators announcing curtailments totaling over half a million tonnes of annual capacity. Others are flexing output to manage power-load price spikes. The resulting supply squeeze is likely to affect aluminium market expectations over the coming days to weeks. | SMM’s daily review says most metals rose, led by stainless steel, with Shanghai aluminium up over 1% for a fourth straight session and aluminium inventories decreasing. Shanghai nickel rose about 2.5% as spot supply remained tight and premiums increased. Copper also edged higher, supporting a bullish near-term tone. | The news says the aluminium queue at Port Klang has fallen to just under 60 days, suggesting congestion is easing and shipments should move more smoothly. With improved logistics and less near-term delivery tightness, market expectations for aluminium availability may turn more favourable over the coming days. | Indonesia’s President said the country will halt bauxite ore exports in 2022 and copper ore exports in 2023 to drive downstream investment and shift exports toward higher-value semi-finished and finished products. Indonesia is a major bauxite and copper exporter, implying reduced global ore supply. | Indonesia’s president said Jakarta plans to stop exporting bauxite ore in 2022 and copper ore in 2023 to accelerate downstream investment and shift toward higher-value products. Indonesia is a major exporter, and the move is expected to affect near-term supply expectations for copper concentrates and aluminium feedstock. | SMM’s afternoon review notes Shanghai nickel rising about 2.56% and Shanghai aluminium gaining roughly 1.1% by midday, while copper also moved higher. For copper, Tianjin epidemic-related disruption left spot trading deserted, with electrolytic copper discounts narrowing. Overall moves appear intraday and mixed across the strip. | Indonesia’s President said the country will stop exporting bauxite ore from 2022 and copper ore in 2023 to accelerate downstream processing and exports of higher-value products. Indonesia already banned nickel ore exports in 2020, signaling a broader shift that could tighten traded ore supplies supporting bearish price pressure. | Indonesia plans to halt bauxite ore exports in 2022 and copper ore exports in 2023 to boost downstream processing and exports of higher-value products. It already banned nickel ore exports from Jan 1, 2020. These actions tighten supply of key raw inputs for aluminum and copper markets.

news_summary: Huafeng Aluminum reported a 2021 pre-increase, with profit for shareholders expected at RMB 430–510 million, up on year due to recovering national demand and rapid new-energy vehicle growth. The company links improved output and sales to demand for aluminum foil and heat-transfer aluminum materials. | SMM reports China electrolytic aluminum inventory falling to 752,000 tons and spot prices for aluminum rising across Guangdong and the Yangtze River. It attributes strength to continued destocking of domestic aluminum ingots and expected supply tightness in Europe from energy-related production disruptions, despite some macro pressure from Fed hawkishness. | SMM reports Shanghai nonferrous metals closed mixed around Jan 11. Nickel led with a 3.45% gain, supported by falling SHFE inventories, LME nickel and tight spot pure-nickel supply. Aluminium rose 1.97% with inventories down, despite weakening demand. Copper was essentially flat, though LME copper inventory fell. | SMM’s daily review reports mixed metal moves in Asia: Shanghai nickel rose more than 3% and stainless steel also jumped, while Shanghai aluminium climbed nearly 2%. Copper edged down slightly (~-0.01%). The piece attributes some nickel support to low inventories and improved spot tightness. | Novelis will invest about $365 million to expand in Todd County, Kentucky, building a new recycling center with 240,000 tons annual capacity and 140 jobs. The project adds sheet ingot casting and shredding and increases recycled aluminium input for automotive markets, implying supportive aluminium supply/demand dynamics.

news_summary: SMM reports sharp rises in aluminium prices, citing higher Guangdong and Yangtze River spot ingot prices. It notes China’s electrolytic aluminium inventories fell to 752,000 tons. The piece attributes support to Europe’s energy-driven production disruptions and ongoing domestic ingot stock build, reinforcing bullish near-term aluminium sentiment. | Nickel prices jumped to a 10-year peak as investors focused on dwindling LME/ShFE inventories, tight nearby supplies (cash premiums), and forecasts of a widening market deficit for EV batteries. The broader industrial metals complex also rose, with copper and aluminium moving higher as supply worries and China stimulus expectations improved sentiment. | Kentucky lawmakers are considering clawing back $15 million of taxpayer funds from a stalled aluminum rolling mill project in northeastern Kentucky. The plant, financed since 2017, still hasn’t broken ground. A bill would require repayment by year-end unless investors are lined up, adding pressure and uncertainty for aluminum capacity timelines. | Kentucky lawmakers are advancing Senate Bill 48 to recoup $15 million of taxpayer funds from a stalled aluminum mill project by Unity Aluminum (formerly Braidy Industries) unless the company repays voluntarily or secures financing. The project has not broken ground after five years, raising production/capacity uncertainty for aluminum.

news_summary: Europe’s energy shortage worsened after Russia cut natural-gas flows to zero via the Yamal-Europe pipeline, raising electricity costs for Western European aluminium smelters. Meanwhile, China’s off-season social aluminium inventory keeps falling, supporting prices despite softer near-term demand. | Antofagasta reported full-year group copper production of 721,500 tonnes, within guidance but 1.7% below last year. The company cites higher costs and guidance for 2022, with copper expected to rise quarter on quarter. | A commentary on fluorspar demand highlights uses in steelmaking and aluminum refining, but the most price-relevant section concerns Alcoa’s San Ciprián aluminum smelter. Workers and the company agreed to curtail the smelter’s 228,000 tpa capacity for two years from early January 2022 due to high energy costs. | The article reports nickel breaking above $23,000/t on the LME, signaling near-term bullish momentum. It also provides context on aluminium market tightness, stating output curtailments in parts of the Gulf and severe shipping disruption through the Strait of Hormuz, though it characterizes aluminium as “hard to move.” | The U.S. and UK will begin talks aimed at resolving their steel and aluminum tariff dispute, including U.S. tariffs on British metals and UK retaliatory tariffs. news_summary: Nickel surged to a 2011 peak on very low exchange inventories, with Russia-related sanctions threats adding supply concerns. Markets also anticipate stronger EV battery demand. LME three-month nickel rose sharply, and traders linked the broader rally to deficits ahead; aluminium was also cited as exposed to supply risk. | China’s primary aluminium imports fell to 82,265 mt in December, down 64.13% MoM and 36.98% YoY, according to customs data. Imports from India also dropped sharply. Reduced primary aluminium inflows to China may tighten available material and provide near-term support for aluminium prices. | Nickel prices surged, helped by LME inventory drawdowns to multi-year lows and expectations of higher EV battery demand. The LME three-month contract hit the highest since 2011, while traders also flagged supply concerns, including a larger projected nickel deficit. Sanctions fears over Russia are also expected to widen aluminium supply gaps. | Jiangsu Haiguang Metal commissioned workshop No. 6 to process aluminum slag/aluminum ash into calcium aluminate, aiming to advance recycling and safe utilization of hazardous waste. The company states large annual processing capacities and output of various aluminum-related materials. The development is supportive for aluminium recycling but not clearly linked to primary metal pricing. | SMM says Shanghai nonferrous metals closed mixed amid US inflation pressure. SHFE copper rose 0.64% as copper inventories fell on the week; aluminium fell 0.28% due to resumed capacity in Yunnan/Qinghai and weaker pre-CNY demand; nickel surged 4.21% on low inventories and backwardation.

news_summary: A Nature Sustainability study suggests scaling photovoltaics to net-zero targets by 2050 could require up to 486 Mt of aluminium, potentially amounting to nearly half of current world production. The aluminum can bottleneck can tighten short-term demand/supply dynamics for aluminium packaging.

news_summary: SMM reports a midday rally in China’s base metals as the US dollar weakened. Shanghai copper rose over 3% to a three-month intraday high, while Shanghai aluminium gained over 2% for the eighth day on tight domestic supply, low inventories and improving downstream spot demand. Shanghai nickel also rose. | SMM’s midday review notes a weaker US dollar and rising industrial metals. Shanghai copper climbed over 3% to a new intraday high for more than three months. Shanghai aluminum rose over 2% for eight straight sessions, supported by tight inventories and expected supply shortages versus imports in the near term. | Shanghai aluminium prices have risen for seven straight sessions as Spring Festival ingot inventory accumulation runs below historical norms. Geopolitical risks from Ukraine/Russia are increasing fears of sanctions and reduced Russian electrolytic-aluminium circulation, while domestic electrolytic output is down and operating rates remain constrained. Demand is mixed but new-energy and foil/strip stay firm. | SMM data shows China’s aluminium ingot social inventory rose to 950,000 mt on Feb 10, increasing by 84,000 mt from Monday. The increase was mainly driven by stocks in Wuxi, Gongyi, Nanhai and Chongqing following Chinese New Year, suggesting more available supply and potentially softer demand. | The piece notes aluminium prices have risen to around $3,300/t even with about 120kt delivered into LME warehouses. It also includes Fastmarkets/Metal Bulletin procedural updates and methodology/publishing corrections, but the headline focus remains the firm aluminium price in the face of warehouse inflows. | LME aluminum prices in February 10 trading rose to about $3,294 per tonne, setting a new all-time high, slightly easing to roughly $3,287 later in the session. news_summary: LME copper three-month futures fell 3.84% to about $9,860/t as markets turned risk-off after US CPI surged, prompting expectations of Fed tightening and lifting the dollar. Aluminium also declined 3.5% to around $3,136/t, despite earlier support from smelter closures and low warehouse inventories. | Sohar Aluminium committed hot metal feedstock to Synergies Casting Oman, a RO 42 million alloy wheel plant under commissioning. Rio Tinto warns escalating tensions could cause disruption to its aluminium operations and supply chain. Authorities signal more sanctions if escalation continues, raising near-term aluminium supply risk expectations. | The article headline suggests Europe is facing acute aluminium tightness, with traders reportedly getting “creative” to source metal. The change implies reduced near-term risk to aluminium trade flows and supply expectations versus prior fears, weighing on the commodity’s outlook over coming days. | Aluminium and nickel prices jumped sharply as Russia escalated its actions in Ukraine, with markets expecting sanctions and supply disruptions. The article notes Russia’s large share of aluminium and nickel production, falling LME inventories, and higher cash premiums, suggesting near-term shortages and upward price pressure for both metals. | Aluminum prices rose about 20% and nickel about 22.7% as Russia escalated actions in eastern Ukraine. Ball Corporation raises minimum can orders, pushing small breweries to pay more via distributors. news_summary: The article headline says the Biden administration team plans to hold off on sanctions that would have targeted aluminum from Russia. If delayed, the change would likely ease near-term supply tightening expectations for Russian-origin metal, supporting a more favorable availability outlook for aluminum markets over coming days. | The news reports an accident at Brazil’s CBA aluminium smelter, but says it will have no impact on production. With aluminium output expected to remain stable, the incident is unlikely to alter near-term supply expectations or move aluminium prices materially. | SMM reports that as Caishui No.40 approaches, throughput of aluminium scrap at Linyi Metals City rose sharply, while downstream processors bought less due to environmental policies. To raise funds and address VAT burdens, local suppliers/traders lowered aluminium scrap prices, squeezing margins and likely dragging recycling prices lower. | SMM reports potential sanctions exemptions for Russian energy and aluminium products amid ongoing Russia-Ukraine tension. Aluminium futures fluctuated, with SHFE staying above 22,700 yuan/mt. Tight supply, rising overseas premiums, and slightly shrinking domestic spot discounts suggest easing stress and supportive near-term sentiment for aluminium. | Vale executives said Russia’s invasion of Ukraine is already driving speculation around nickel prices and could influence iron ore pellet prices. The company also discussed longer-term demand: copper demand may rise ~30% by the next decade. However, the piece gives no clear near-term directional price move for copper or aluminium.

news_summary: Western sanctions against Russia intensified after bank blocking from SWIFT, raising fears of disrupted Russian exports and inputs. Russia is a key aluminum producer and supplier of gas used in aluminum production, and also has meaningful nickel mine supply. LME three-month aluminum hit a record while nickel and copper rose. | LME aluminum prices in trading rose above $3,500 per tonne, setting a new historical maximum. By 9:55 Moscow time, the contract was around $3,520.5. The prior record had been reached on February 24, indicating renewed strong upside momentum. | Russia-Ukraine tensions escalated, lifting crude oil and worsening Europe’s natural gas/energy crisis. Higher energy costs suppressed European aluminium plant supply and supported LME aluminium, which rose over 3% to $3,470/mt. LME inventories destocked, while domestic production resumed but total output remained below last year. | Velocity Ventures bought the former Aluminum Shapes complex for $32 million at auction and received planning approval to resume aluminum production at a Pennsauken (Delair) plant. The renewed operations are expected to occupy only part of the site, with about 200 jobs and remaining space marketed for warehousing. | The report says aluminium is rallying after Rusal halted shipments at a key Ukrainian plant. By disrupting near-term export supply, the move is expected to tighten physical availability and raise market expectations for aluminium prices over the coming days. | Wood Mackenzie/Reuters reports Rusal has halted alumina shipments from its Nikolaev refinery near Mykolaiv after Russia’s invasion and new sanctions. Nikolaev alumina feeds Rusal smelters in Russia, and diversion or failed counterparties could disrupt overseas alumina supplies, worsening aluminium market tightness amid an expected deficit. | The piece reports that Russian metal exports are sliding as sanctions interfere with commodity financing. Reduced access to trade finance and logistics can limit prompt shipments, tightening near-term supply expectations for industrial metals like copper, nickel, and aluminium and supporting bullish price pressure. | Aluminium prices surged to a record on the LME after Western nations expanded sanctions on Russia, raising supply disruption worries. Additional banking restrictions and Rusal halting alumina shipments from its Nikolaev refinery pressured the market. The piece also flags energy and sanctions spillovers that can affect other industrial metals.

news_summary: LME aluminium rebounded after losses as uncertainty over Russian supply increased volatility. Aluminium for three-month delivery rose 2.59% to $3,427.5/t, with Russia contributing ~6% of global supply. Wider Russian supply disruptions and market actions affected other metals, while nickel trading was suspended and copper spot was discounted versus forwards. | The piece argues Lun (LME-linked) aluminium has surged on higher European energy costs, Western Europe production reduction announcements, and restrictions on Russian exports after Rusal payment disruptions. It says Shanghai aluminium is weaker due to softer domestic demand but export profits and active overseas orders should support recovery. | SMM’s March outlook says SHFE aluminium and spot prices trended with intra-month volatility, ending Feb up around 2–3%. Supply rose on production resumption in multiple regions but was partly offset by a Guangxi pandemic-triggered enterprise shutdown. Downstream operating rates improved post–Spring Festival, while social inventories grew more slowly. | Rio Tinto plans to suspend bauxite shipments to Russia-owned Aughinish Alumina refinery in Ireland following Russia’s invasion of Ukraine and steps to sever ties with Russian businesses. Bauxite feedstock is exchanged for alumina under an existing agreement. After the news, aluminium prices reportedly rose on the LME. | Aluminium prices were volatile and down about 10% on the week despite a Friday rise, after uncertainty over Russian exports and a broader Ukraine-related supply shock drove earlier record highs. Higher energy/coal costs threaten smelter output, while LME inventories and physical premiums tighten conditions, sustaining volatility. Nickel trading remains disrupted. | Dozens of major firms are exiting or scaling back operations in Russia. In the energy and metals section, Rio Tinto says it is terminating commercial relationships with Russian businesses, including its Queensland Alumina/Rusal aluminium-related joint venture. The move could disrupt aluminium/alumina supply and support higher prices. | Shanghai aluminum trended up as spot sentiment strengthened after suspension of production at Rusal’s Ukrainian alumina plant and tightening sanctions-related expectations, though the article says UK sanctions’ immediate impact is relatively small. Demand rebounded after the price fall, inventories fell, and supply was stable, reinforcing bullish short-term bias. | Aluminium is set for a big weekly fall, down nearly 10% despite a Friday rise, driven by volatility after uncertainty over Russian exports. Russia’s war-related supply shock, tighter inventories, higher premiums, and energy-cost risks for smelters are cited. Copper and nickel dynamics are also referenced via stocks and suspension after a surge.

news_summary: Oil falls and risk appetite improves as markets react to Russia-Ukraine peace talks and easing inflation expectations. A key focus is nickel: LME trading resumes within 24 hours after a one-week halt, with new rules including daily price limits and position disclosures. China lockdowns and softer demand weigh on commodities. | SMM reports aluminium prices were relatively stable in mid-March, with thinner scrap transaction volumes as recyclers showed limited willingness to ship during renewed COVID controls in China. Pandemic measures disrupted domestic shipments and reduced downstream demand, while spot/quoted scrap prices largely held steady despite prior aluminium declines. | Most base metals rose as traders weighed supply risks and hopes for additional stimulus in China. LME copper and aluminium were up early in the session. Nickel’s LME trading is set to resume after its March 8 suspension following a rapid price spike, potentially capping near-term moves. | Reuters reports Rusal diverted bauxite ore shipments from Guinea to its Ireland refining facility after shelling shut its Nikolaev facility. The firm says exports from Guinea are temporarily cut due to the Ukraine crisis. Aughinish is Rusal’s largest European alumina plant, with processing capacity stated in the article. | SMM reports that LME aluminum inventories continued to fall on March 16, declining by 8,300 tons, with most of the withdrawal occurring from Asian warehouses. The drawdown suggests tighter available supply on exchange and can support short-term expectations for higher aluminum prices. | LME nickel resumed trading after a one-week suspension and fell 5% to its lower limit amid thin liquidity and more sellers than buyers. LME aluminium edged down 0.5%, while copper climbed 1.5% on hopes for further China stimulus and policy steps to keep markets stable, alongside trade-news support for metals. | LME nickel resumed after a week off and slid 5% to its lower limit amid thin trading and more sellers than buyers. Copper gained 1.5% on hopes for additional China stimulus. Benchmark aluminium eased about 0.5%. China policy comments and nickel swap/production news shaped sentiment. | A canner warns that increases in energy and inputs could raise costs substantially. It notes that aluminium used for cans is produced mainly in Russia and Ukraine, where supply is uncertain, and that can prices have already increased. The company is buying in advance but price stability is not guaranteed.

news_summary: The US will lift Trump-era steel and aluminum tariffs on UK imports, removing Section 232 duties on UK aluminium and allowing sustainably based volumes into the US. The UK will also drop retaliatory tariffs. This should add near-term aluminium supply and weigh on prices; copper effects are indirect. | A report by the International Aluminium Institute (IAI) says global aluminium demand could rise nearly 40% by 2030, requiring 33.3 Mt more production. Growth drivers include EVs, solar power, construction and sustainable packaging, with most incremental demand expected from China and Asia. | The US and UK agreed to end Trump-era tariffs on steel and aluminum imports, removing the UK’s retaliatory tariffs and allowing duty-free flows into the US. Officials framed the deal as easing supply-demand gaps and aiding domestic producers, though it may also increase import supply pressures for aluminium prices. | China’s aluminum product exports rose sharply from January to February, with higher shipments of unwrought aluminum and key downstream items like strips/foil, despite some declines in profiles and certain categories. It cites epidemic and energy shortages boosting reliance on Chinese supplies and notes a strong rise in export values, supporting aluminium price sentiment. | The US and UK agreed to end tariffs on imported British steel and aluminum that were imposed during the Trump era. The move is intended to resolve a bilateral trade dispute, with the UK also lifting retaliatory tariffs on US exports. Annual audits are required for certain Chinese-owned UK steel. | The U.S. and Britain agreed to end a long-running dispute by allowing specified volumes of British aluminium (and steel) to enter the U.S. without Section 232 tariffs. The deal also removes related retaliatory tariffs, easing trade frictions and likely improving near-term aluminium supply expectations and price support. | The U.S. and Britain reached a trade deal to end a long-running dispute over Section 232 tariffs on steel and aluminum. The deal allows tariff-free, historically based volumes of British steel and aluminium to enter the U.S., lifting retaliatory tariffs and easing inflation concerns, benefiting U.S. consumers and producers. | London aluminium prices edged higher as Russia-Ukraine-related supply concerns increased, supported by Australia’s ban on exports of aluminium-making materials to Russia and output cuts by European smelters facing higher energy costs. It notes LME has no plans to ban Russian metals, while copper and nickel were weaker overall. | The US and Britain reached a trade deal ending a Section 232 tariff dispute on steel and aluminum. British aluminium products will enter the US without the 10% tariff, and retaliatory tariffs on over $500 million of US exports to Britain are lifted. The change should support near-term aluminium market sentiment. | Britain said it resolved a US trade dispute that had left UK exporters facing steel and aluminium tariffs. Under the deal, UK aluminium and steel exporters get high-level tariff-free access to the US, taking effect June 1, after the US lifted Trump-era measures for the UK. | SMM reports aluminium prices rose early week, but aluminium scrap prices (taint and tense) showed mixed, mostly smaller changes. Supply in scrap was tight and transactions remained weak due to pandemic-related transport restrictions and required nucleic acid testing. Trading may improve after restrictions ease, but near-term activity is muted. | BNN Bloomberg reports that Indian aluminum mills are getting the fewest coal rail rakes in six months. The shortage of coal transport likely limits fuel availability for smelters, potentially constraining aluminum output in the near term and weighing on aluminium price expectations over the coming days. | The article headline says the US has lifted tariffs on imported UK steel and aluminium products, including boats. While steel is not tracked here, removing aluminium-related tariffs should lower import costs and support near-term aluminium supply and demand expectations, providing a mild bullish impetus for aluminium prices. | Australia immediately banned alumina exports to Russia, cutting off a major share of Russia-bound alumina feedstock used to produce aluminum. Rusal faces supply-chain disruptions and possible production impacts, while the article reports LME aluminum prices rising about 5.7% to over $3,500/ton, with further inflation fears. | EU regulators are considering a sharp rise in duties on Russian aluminium imports. With Russian metal a significant share of Europe’s primary supply and limited substitution, buyers would likely absorb higher costs. Other producers could lift prices, using the apparent supply deficit, pushing aluminium prices up for European consumers and industry.

news_summary: SMM market tracking reports broad weakness in China’s non-ferrous complex early March 30: Shanghai nickel fell 2.75% and Shanghai aluminum dropped 1.5% (inner market), while Shanghai copper declined slightly. Overseas trade improves marginally for some contracts, but epidemic/logistics frictions and shifting macro sentiment keep pressure on metals near term. | Overnight SHFE and LME aluminium fell sharply after opening higher, with LME down 4.33% on the day. Easing Russia-Ukraine tensions and macro guidance were bearish. Fundamentals were mixed: new capacity ramped up, transport remained restricted, while LME stocks declined 7,800 mt. Overall sentiment turned bearish for aluminium. | MoHUA officials flagged off India’s first aluminium-body metro train for Maha Metro’s Pune project. The coaches use lighter aluminium car bodies (about 6.5% lighter than stainless steel), with 34 trains and 102 coaches planned. Speakers cite energy efficiency, longer life, and Make-in-India/Atma Nirbhar goals. | Rusal said 2021 earnings rose due to higher LME aluminium prices, but new sanctions on Russia and tighter trade (including an Australian real-time export ban on alumina/alumina ores to Russia) may disrupt operations. Rusal also temporarily closed its Nikolaev alumina refinery, raising supply-risk for aluminium/alumina. | LME aluminium rebounded after a near 5% prior-session drop as optimism around Russia-Ukraine talks faded, reducing a supply-related risk premium. A softer U.S. dollar supported metals, but China demand worries from a COVID surge capped gains. Russia sanctions remain a key supply overhang for aluminium, nickel and copper. | Rusal warned that Russia’s economic isolation could disrupt raw-material supply and markets, citing Australia’s ban on sending alumina to Russia (covering about 20% of Rusal demand) and a shutdown of its Ukrainian alumina plant. Uncertainty remains over Aughinish, with potential investment delays and higher financing costs. | SMM data show LME aluminum inventories fell by 8,750 tons on March 30, with the largest decline in the Klang warehouse. Lower exchange stocks can signal tighter near-term supply and tighter prompt availability, generally supportive for aluminum prices over the following days. | Reuters reports Rusal’s bauxite shipments from Guinea have ground to a halt as the Ukraine war disrupts operations and refinery access. With Australia banning exports to Russia and the Nikolaev alumina refinery shuttered, bauxite is diverted or stockpiled, tightening feed supply and likely boosting aluminium prices. | Reuters reports Rusal’s Guinea bauxite shipments have stopped amid Russia-Ukraine war disruptions and trade backlash. The outage affects bauxite supply to Rusal’s refineries (notably Aughinish) and coincides with Australia banning bauxite/alumina exports to Russia. With feed shortages likely, aluminium output could face near-term constraints. | SMM reports aluminium prices moved slightly (down then up then down) over three days, while aluminium scrap prices stayed largely unchanged because recyclers and deliveries were constrained by local pandemic conditions and invoice/tax policy considerations. Scrap trading volumes fell sharply in Shandong, suggesting disruptions but minimal net price direction. | The article’s headline says billionaire Birla will invest $7.2 billion tied to an aluminium demand bet. Such large, stated demand-focused spending can raise near-term expectations for aluminium consumption and tighten sentiment around supply-demand balance, supporting aluminium prices despite limited specifics in the provided excerpt. | Aluminium prices jumped as markets renewed fears of Russian supply disruptions and the dollar weakened. The story cites Russia’s role in aluminium, copper, and nickel supply, potential sanctions and transport issues, and high European gas prices that have forced smelters to cut output. LME aluminium, copper, and nickel rose. | Ball Corporation and Resource Recycling Systems report that raising U.S. aluminum can recycling to 90% would generate jobs, cut emissions, and keep about 1.3 million tons of aluminum out of landfills. The study also claims higher recycled supply would reduce dependence on primary aluminum imports, supporting aluminum availability. | The article highlights that the US copper premium has reached a historic high, pointing to tighter physical pricing conditions and supportive near-term copper sentiment. It also references Fastmarkets’ aluminium differential/premium assessment corrections and notes periods of disrupted regional supply/shipping, which may influence aluminium differentials.

news_summary: LME aluminium closed lower on the day but posted the biggest quarterly gain since 1988, driven by supply disruptions and higher smelting costs tied to Russia-Ukraine conflict and sanctions. Nickel also surged strongly after recent volatility. The piece adds supportive copper signals, including an export license and weaker Chile output. | SMM says domestic aluminium prices face downward pressure. Costs are stable (energy policy-controlled, alumina weak) while smelter profits are high, lifting operating rates and output. Imports are effectively closed and pandemic-hit demand limits consumption. Despite Europe’s energy crisis supporting LME, SHFE is expected to underperform and inventories may rise. | Copper dropped as China’s factory activity data signaled sharper deterioration, increasing near-term demand concerns. A firmer U.S. dollar further pressured dollar-priced metals. Investors stayed cautious ahead of U.S. jobs data. Other base metals weakened too, with aluminium slightly lower and nickel down on Shanghai trading. | Copper prices fell 1.1% as investors worried China’s weak factory activity and Shanghai COVID lockdowns would curb demand. LME three-month copper retreated to about $10,264/t. The article cites supply/energy and other metal moves, but copper remains the clearest downside read. | Washington officials approved funding to upgrade the idled Alcoa Intalco aluminum smelter, but restart hinges on securing affordable electricity from BPA. BPA says it lacks available power and cannot meet desired reduced rates. Continued delays keep supply constrained, while any potential restart depends on power contracts. | Washington officials secured $10 million for Intalco smelter upgrades, but restart hinges on obtaining affordable electricity from BPA. The agency declined reduced-rate power, prompting ongoing talks and debate over hydropower allocation. Supporters say restarting would restore aluminum supply and “green” output, while utilities oppose a subsidy.

news_summary: London copper rose for a second session as traders worried about supply from Chile after February output fell 7.5%. Gains were tempered by a firmer U.S. dollar and lingering concerns over Chinese demand, with China holiday/lockdown measures affecting sentiment. LME also plans an independent review into last month’s nickel chaos.

The overseas-China price spread narrowed, reducing exporter profits, while domestic alumina stayed mostly stable. | SMM’s March composite PMI for China’s aluminium downstream processing came in at 49.1 (down y/y), reflecting COVID-related logistics and production disruptions, lower-than-expected operating rates, and weaker orders. Several segments remain in contraction, with rising finished-product inventories. Easing is hoped in April, but near-term demand looks soft. | LME data show aluminium inventories fell further after a prior surge, reaching 623,325 mt and a new 16-year low. SHFE inventories in Shanghai also declined for a third straight week, down 1.02% to 305,805 mt. The falling stocks suggest tighter near-term availability for aluminium. | SMM reports Chinese aluminium smelters lifted April prebaked anode bids by 850 yuan/mt, pushing total smelter costs up to 18,317 yuan/mt from last week. Meanwhile aluminium prices fell, cutting profits. While coal prices eased in some regions, cost volatility from alumina, freight rates, and anodes remains high. | SMM reports that LME aluminum inventories fell by 3,450 tons on April 6, marking the largest drop in the Klang warehouse. Lower visible exchange stocks can signal tighter near-term availability and improve market sentiment for aluminium prices. | Oleg Deripaska, a major shareholder in Aughinish Alumina via Rusal, is under consideration for EU sanctions. The EU is also preparing wider measures against Russia, including bans on certain imports and restrictions on banks, vessels, and procurement. Such steps could disrupt Russia-linked alumina/aluminium supply. | The article reports Trump plans 25% tariffs on steel and 10% tariffs on aluminum starting next week, with markets falling sharply on the policy. Aluminum is directly targeted, likely tightening import supply. The broader risk-off tone could weigh on industrial demand, creating offsetting forces for copper. | EU is considering sanctions on Oleg Deripaska, a major shareholder in Aughinish Alumina via Rusal, alongside broader measures targeting Russia’s financial sector and trade. Proposed bans on coal, limits on bank transactions, port access restrictions, and potential further action on fossil-fuel revenues increase supply-chain and risk concerns for industrial metals. | China’s aluminium ingot social inventory rose to 1.05 million mt on Apr 6, up 13,000 mt week-on-week. Growth came mainly from Wuxi after buyers couldn’t collect cargos due to truck shipment hindrances. SMM links the rise to reduced transport efficiency during the pandemic and will monitor effects on prices. | Fastmarkets opened an IOSCO-compliant consultation to review methodologies used for publishing aluminium premiums, including MB-AL-0302 and MB-AL-0300 6063 extrusion billet premiums (DDP terms) and the MB-AL-0316 aluminium P1020A premium. The update adds an Italy FCA premium as part of the audited pricing framework. | Reuters reports Rusal exported its first Guinea bauxite shipment in nearly a month, after disruption from the war in Ukraine. Using shipments via Kamsar port, ore feeds Rusal’s Aughinish refinery in Ireland. With Australia having banned bauxite exports to Russia, any resumed Guinea supply supports aluminium refining availability. | The article links a growing aluminium can “can-demic” to higher demand from craft beer and pet food packaging alongside tighter supply. It notes China cutting production and suppliers raising purchase minimums, leaving smaller firms relying on third parties. Can prices nearly doubled pre-pandemic, suggesting bullish aluminium price pressure.

Activists welcomed the move, reducing uncertainty for aluminium-related inputs. | Bunting will open a new aluminum extrusion mill in Pennsylvania, redeveloping a large facility and investing in equipment and workforce via state incentives. The expansion targets construction, automotive, and transportation markets, suggesting incremental aluminum consumption from extrusion production. | SMM said China aluminium social inventory reached 1.07 million mt as of April 7, up 25,000 mt on the week. Other regions were mostly flat or down, including Nanhai (-7,000 mt). | DADA Holdings, Concord Resources and Italy’s Invitalia acquired the business unit of Slim Fusina Rolling in Venice, Italy, including a vertically integrated cast house and rolling mills. The operation has about 75,000 t/year rolling capacity, and production and deliveries were maintained through its prior insolvency process. | Shanghai aluminium prices fell to a three-week low as fresh China lockdowns and expectations of tighter monetary policy raised worries about weaker growth and metals demand. A stronger dollar pressured dollar-priced commodities, pulling LME and ShFE copper lower too, with nickel trading/infrastructure concerns adding sentiment drag. | SMM data shows LME aluminium inventories fell by 4,025 tons on April 8, with the biggest reductions in the Klang and Singapore warehouses. news_summary: Culpeo Minerals completed induced polarisation surveys at its Quelon iron oxide-copper-gold project in Chile, identifying large geophysical anomalies and stating three zones are compelling drill targets. The company is pursuing earn-in stakes and plans a maiden drilling program. The update is exploratory and does not change near-term copper supply. | LME copper rises 1.7% to a two-week peak as low inventories and China stimulus hopes coincide with supply worries. MMG’s Las Bambas mine plans to suspend operations after a local protest begins. Aluminium stabilizes near $3,214 per tonne on technical support, suggesting near-term resilience. | LME data show aluminum inventories fell again after a prior spike, with last week’s level reaching the lowest in 17 years. SHFE aluminum inventories also decreased 2.46% week-on-week to 299,855 mt, the lowest in two months. The inventory draw suggests tightening supply and price support for aluminum. | Portugal will introduce a contribution from 1 July 2022 on disposable plastic or multi-material food packages and in 2023 on packages made of aluminium or multi-material with aluminium. The charge is paid by producers/importers via a customs/e-DIC process and is meant to be passed to final consumers, potentially weighing on aluminium packaging demand.
