news_summary: London copper edged up as hopes for additional China stimulus offset a firm dollar and growth concerns. A suspension at Peru’s Las Bambas mine added a copper supply-tightness risk. Aluminium and nickel in LME and Shanghai were broadly softer, reflecting uneven sentiment tied to China lockdown-related demand worries. | China’s alumina exports to Russia rose more than 90x in March after sanctions over Ukraine reduced Russia’s traditional supply routes. Shipments to Russia reached 9,949 tonnes, while total alumina exports jumped 180% year-on-year. Australia’s alumina and aluminium-ore ban to Russia is cited as the driver. | China’s primary aluminium imports fell 55.1% year-on-year in March to 39,433 mt, though they rose 115% month-on-month. Q1 imports totaled 96,551.5 mt, down 71% year-on-year. The shift in import flows could influence short-term aluminium supply expectations. | SMM reports early-session SHFE aluminium plate trading around 21,800 yuan/mt with narrowed ranges. Spot ingot transactions in East China stayed slightly discounted, reflecting cautious longs and weaker holder pricing after declines. Demand improved modestly with downstream pandemic recovery, but upstream refined output resumed, adding supply pressure. | SMM reports a deal for 30,000 mt of alumina sold at $371/mt FOB Western Australia, down $7/mt from the prior transaction. The shipment is scheduled for late May and the destination is undisclosed. The pricing drop suggests weaker near-term alumina market sentiment, a key input for aluminium smelting. | Grand View Research says the global aluminum casting market should grow to $135.2B by 2030, citing rising demand for lightweight vehicles and equipment. It expects steady use of aluminum components, despite some impact from electrification. China’s scale in primary aluminum and manufacturing growth underpin demand for casting inputs. | Tight aluminium availability pushed Rotterdam duty-unpaid premiums to an all-time high near $500/t, alongside stable but elevated Midwest US premiums at record levels. LME warehouse stocks fell and energy costs remained high, while sanctions-fear around Russian supply supported demand for physical material. | Hawaiian Airlines will replace plastic water bottles on certain routes with Mananalu’s 16-ounce BPA-free aluminum bottles, aiming to remove about 142,000 plastic bottles annually from transpacific operations. The move increases demand for recycled aluminum packaging but offers limited, localized market impact. | Kaiser Aluminum reported Q1 2022 results with strong demand in general engineering and packaging and improving aerospace recovery, while automotive remained muted. Higher freight and inflationary costs were partially offset by contained-metal surcharges. It also cited lingering supply-chain issues affecting magnesium and timely metal supply at its Warrick facility. | Kaiser Aluminum reported Q1 profit of $8 million (EPS $0.51), up from $5 million a year earlier and matching analysts’ expectations (adjusted EPS $0.66). Revenue rose sharply to $949 million from $324 million. With results inline with estimates, the impact on near-term aluminium price expectations appears limited. | The article headline says Alcoa’s aluminum shipments are declining, a sign that demand is waning. news_summary: Novelis will invest over $30 million in Germany to add a continuous annealing line at its Plettenberg-Ohle facility, doubling capacity for aluminium used in coffee capsules. The expansion targets growing demand for sustainable, high-recycled-content packaging and supports recycled aluminium supply from its Ohle and Nachterstedt operations. | China imported 120,600 mt of unwrought aluminium alloy in March, up 54.5% YoY and 31.3% MoM. Customs data also shows strong Q1 growth. The report links expectations of aluminium supply shortage to a deepening overseas energy crisis, supporting LME aluminium and lifting ADC12 prices, with fewer new orders. | SMM says China’s aluminium ingot social inventory fell to 1.02 million mt on April 21, down 42,000 mt week over week, helped by higher warehouse shipments and stronger demand in some regions. Poor transport temporarily lifted Wuxi inventories, while improved post-pandemic conditions boosted downstream restocking. | SMM said China’s aluminium billet inventory fell to 119,100 mt as of Apr 21, down 11.37% from a week earlier, with declines across major regions. Logistics and pandemic controls continue to weigh on transport efficiency, while destocking and rigid/seasonal demand support expectations of further inventory reductions into Labour Day.

news_summary: SMM says operating rates for major aluminium processing enterprises fell 3.3 percentage points to 64.5% as East China’s pandemic situation suppressed demand. Downstream firms cut production, finished-product inventories rose, and transportation disruptions made it harder to source inputs and ship output. Rates could rebound if infections ease. | LME copper is on track for a third weekly decline as China’s COVID lockdowns cloud demand, while LME-approved inventories have risen to the highest since last October. Rising inventories and a stronger U.S. dollar add pressure. Limited support comes from hopes of Chinese stimulus and supply disruptions, but overall bias remains down. | Fastmarkets proposes launching a monthly bauxite CIF China price assessment (from accepted origins including Guinea, Australia, Indonesia, and Brazil) and discontinuing existing FOB bauxite assessments for Guinea and Brazil, subject to market feedback. The change is methodological/benchmarking, not an explicit physical market shock. | Citigroup downgraded Alcoa to neutral from buy, citing aluminium price assumptions (around $2,800 mid-spot versus a long-term $2,500) and a more balanced near-term risk-reward after a large stock rally. The move reflects expectations for aluminium rather than a clear new supply or demand shock. | ArcWest Exploration provided an exploration update for its Oxide Peak Cu-Au project, including planned 2022 drilling of an untested porphyry target and prior geophysical/geochemical results indicating copper anomalies. The release is company/project specific with no immediate changes to mine output, inventories, or pricing mechanisms for copper, nickel, or aluminium.

Despite some relief from falling thermal coal, the article warns aluminium costs are unlikely to decline due to potential increases in alumina and anode prices, plus higher freight rates. | Industrial metals fell as China’s COVID-19 lockdown restrictions raised fears of weaker demand and economic growth. Copper hit its lowest level in over a month, while expectations for larger Fed rate hikes supported a stronger dollar, pressuring dollar-priced metals. Additional headlines include a negative outlook for copper projects and Peru mine restart. | China imported 11.7 million mt of bauxite in March, a record and mostly from Guinea, as Indonesia’s export ban uncertainty and rainy-season disruptions keep Indonesian supply unstable. Chinese alumina refineries shifted toward Guinean ore to maintain production and stockpiles, supporting more stable alumina supply and potentially aluminium output. | SMM reports China’s aluminium operating capacity climbed in Q1 with higher operating rates and resumption of previously shut plants, and expects further capacity growth in Q2 to about 40.94 million mt. It also cites capacity replacements reducing net output less than planned, implying supply support and potential near-term price pressure. | Yunnan Statistics Bureau data cited by SMM show aluminium output rose to 377,000 mt in March, up 43.6% month-on-month. By end-March, around 80% of aluminium capacity in the province had been released, implying increased near-term supply and potential pressure on aluminium prices. | China’s aluminum plate/sheet and strip exports rose in March, but companies report April export orders have slumped month-on-month. They attribute weaker shipments partly to pandemic disruptions and also to improving overseas capacity in Western countries as COVID-19 restrictions ease, reducing dependence on Chinese supply. | Vedanta Aluminium’s Lanjigarh unit in Odisha runs a week-long malaria awareness and prevention campaign for local communities, citing reduced malaria cases from prior efforts. The piece mainly covers health outreach and surveillance/testing improvements, with only background details on Vedanta’s alumina refinery and aluminium smelting operations. | Europe’s aluminium semis market is described as extremely tight due to tariffs and sanctions limiting Russian deliveries, with further EU restrictions (including possible import/payments limits) under consideration. At the same time, China is expanding semi-finished output and improving primary smelting, making it the key swing supplier. Premiums remain high.

news_summary: London copper rebounded 1.4% after hitting a more-than-two-month low, driven by hopes for additional China economic stimulus and a slight retreat in the U.S. dollar. The piece also notes lingering COVID lockdown worries and broader macro uncertainty, with aluminium and nickel referenced via markets and related company updates. | CME Group said it will launch a COMEX-listed aluminum option contract on May 23, pending regulatory approval. The exchange highlighted higher aluminum futures trading volumes and positioned the new contract as additional risk-management and hedging flexibility. The announcement is about derivatives access, not immediate changes to aluminum fundamentals. | The article reviews copper market drivers: a still-large but narrowing refined deficit, Chinese demand slowing tied to real estate weakness, European consumption pressure from high energy costs, and uncertainty from Russia-related sanctions and financing. It also notes limited copper-aluminium substitution, leaving copper volatility high but directionally mixed. | This podcast episode reviews Alcoa’s earnings call and the company’s weaker stock reaction. Speakers cited rising energy costs, disruptions tied to leaving Russia, shipping challenges, and environmental initiatives toward zero-carbon smelters. The operational and cost pressures discussed are likely to weigh on near-term aluminium supply expectations.

news_summary: The EU’s proposed batteries regulation would require battery makers and importers to source bauxite (linked to aluminium) and copper responsibly, if included in the final list of regulated raw materials. A coalition argues the rules should cover bauxite, copper, and iron. Negotiations are ongoing with differing positions in EU institutions. | An EU batteries regulation proposal would require battery importers and manufacturers to ensure responsible sourcing of key raw materials used in batteries. A coalition argues the final list should include bauxite (for aluminum) and copper, alongside iron, citing human-rights and environmental risks in mining and refining. | SMM reports Shanghai-area aluminium billet social inventories at 105,900 mt on Apr 28, down 9.88% from a week earlier. Inventory fell across most major regions as downstream producers restocked ahead of the Labour Day holiday, though sluggish demand in Foshan slowed the decline. Stocks are expected to drop further after the holiday. | CRISIL Research forecasts aluminium prices to average about 25% higher year-on-year, linked to LME contract pricing. Despite recent declines from conflict-era highs, the report expects a supply deficit in 2022 supported by extended cuts and robust demand, with demand and export dynamics limiting downside. | The article says aluminium prices rose to ₹300/kg in March but are likely to ease in the second half of the fiscal due to excess supply. It forecasts average domestic prices around ₹225–235/kg and links these to three-month LME contracts, where March’s average was $3,537/tonne. | CME says it will launch options on aluminium physical futures after successful physical trades in March and April. It cites record aluminium futures volumes and rising client use, positioning CME as an alternative to the LME. The move supports aluminium hedging and liquidity, potentially providing modest near-term upside sentiment. | Century Aluminum reported Q1 2022 results highlighting higher shipments and net sales driven by higher LME aluminum prices. It cited a global ~1 million tonne aluminum supply deficit and the lowest LME inventory levels since 2005, plus restart projects at Mt. Holly and Hawesville, supporting tightening sentiment for aluminum. | Century Aluminum Co reported a sharp Q1 improvement, with earnings rising to $16.6 million from a loss a year earlier and revenue increasing to $753.6 million. Adjusted earnings were $60.7 million ($0.59 per share). The update can affect near-term sentiment on aluminium producers.

news_summary: SMM says China’s eight major markets recorded aluminium ingot social inventories of 1.03 million mt as of May 5, up 46,000 mt week-on-week but down 82,000 mt year-on-year. Holiday arrivals lifted inventories in Gongyi and Nanhai; logistics restrictions eased. Suppliers face selling pressure, while downstream buying stays cautious, weighing on aluminium prices. | SMM reports aluminium futures have fallen sharply on the week amid a likely US rate hike, stronger USD and weaker risk appetite. Downstream operating rates remain low with order declines in auto and real estate. Although inventories dropped, they fell due to delayed arrivals, so higher post-holiday arrivals may raise stocks and pressure prices. | SMM reports domestic alumina prices were flat week-on-week despite easier north-China transport after improved pandemic conditions and some COVID recurrence in Guangxi. Smelters stockpiled on logistics concerns, but weak aluminium prices and adequate long-term supply muted upward momentum. Support may be temporary, while future oversupply and imports loom. | SMM reports aluminium smelters’ full costs eased slightly but profits fell sharply as aluminium prices declined. Thermal coal costs dropped after cancelled import tariffs, while power tariffs were mostly stable short term. However, prebaked anode prices are seen rising in May and freight rates increased, implying higher aluminium costs ahead. | SMM reports aluminium prices lost momentum through April, with SHFE and LME both falling. It attributes weakness to rising domestic supply from capacity restarts, muted downstream demand/PMI and higher inventories due to pandemic logistics, plus bearish sentiment from US rate hikes. Aluminium is expected to stay pressured in May. | China’s domestic aluminium billet inventory rose 22,700 mt to 128,600 mt on May 5 after Labour Day. Large arrivals from Yunnan and stagnant trading boosted stocks, especially in Foshan. Downstream buying stayed cautious due to ongoing consumption disruption/pandemic rumours, creating near-term inventory pressure and price downside risk as buyers wait.

news_summary: Aluminium futures and LME prices fell over May 5-6. SMM data show social aluminium inventories rising and spot premiums weakening, with domestic downstream procurement described as weak and processing operating rates down. Despite some shipments and faster plant resumption, the net view is that aluminium fundamentals remain pressured and prices may stay rangebound at low. | SMM says major aluminium processing operating rates fell 7.5 percentage points to 57.3% week-on-week, hurt by Labour Day shutdowns and pandemic disruptions. Extrusion and secondary producers saw the sharpest declines, with higher transport costs and inventory build. Rates may improve only as holiday effects fade and pandemic improves. | The article reports a record high for the aluminium industry in 2021, attributed to sustained recovery of demand in Europe. It notes deliveries rising 3.9% while exports fell 15%, implying stronger domestic/contract demand versus weaker outward shipments, which is generally supportive for near-term aluminium pricing sentiment. | SMM reports aluminium prices fell 420 yuan/mt to 20,300 yuan/mt. Corresponding aluminium scrap prices also dropped: extrusion scrap by 200–300 yuan/mt and tense scrap by 100–200 yuan/mt, with some areas like Shandong seeing declines up to 400 yuan/mt. Drops varied by local COVID severity. | Copper, aluminium, nickel and other commodities have fallen as China tightened Covid controls. The lockdowns reduce activity in end-use industries, cutting near-term metal demand and weighing on broader sentiment toward the metals complex. The price declines over the past couple of weeks reflect this demand shock. | Copper, aluminium, nickel and other metals have fallen over the past couple of weeks as China tightened Covid curbs. The lockdowns reduce demand from the world’s largest metal consumer and dampen sentiment toward the metal complex, creating negative price pressure across industrial commodities in the near term. | Shanghai’s Covid-19 controls affected bonded metals flows. Copper bonded stocks kept increasing in April but more slowly as transport improved and import arbitrage turned profitable. Aluminium and nickel bonded stocks declined due to closed or loss-making arbitrage windows and limited inflows/outflows, with weak trading and constrained logistics.

news_summary: SMM data show China’s April aluminium output rose to 3.298 mln mt and capacity utilization stayed high. Demand was weaker in April due to repeated COVID-related logistics issues, leaving delayed ingot arrivals and a clearer backlog. May output and capacity are expected to rise, but improved transport could lift downstream use and reduce inventories later. | SMM says Linyi (Shandong) had strict pandemic freight controls since April, reducing aluminium scrap supply, but shipments rose after the Ministry of Transport allowed compliant drivers to pass through. Despite eased transport elsewhere, recyclers are cautious for May due to lower prices and demand and higher inflows of non-standard ADC12 secondary aluminium. | China’s customs data show total exports and imports rising year-on-year. Specifically, exports of unwrought aluminium and aluminium semis increased 29.2% in Jan–Apr, while copper ore and concentrates imports rose 4.5% over the same period. These trade-flow shifts can influence short-term supply expectations for aluminium and copper. | China’s April exports of unwrought aluminium and aluminium semis were about 596,900 mt, largely unchanged MoM but up sharply YoY, with COVID-impacted transport limiting further gains. April export profits improved as the SHFE/LME ratio recovered. With logistics improving, May exports are expected to stay up about 35% YoY. | SMM reported China’s aluminium ingot social inventories at 1.03 million mt on May 9, down 3,000 mt from a week earlier and 72,000 mt versus a year earlier. Some regions rose (e.g., Gongyi) on delayed arrivals, while others fell (e.g., Wuxi, Hangzhou) as arrivals slowed. | SMM says SHFE aluminium slid to a four-and-a-half-month low near 19,575 yuan/mt after weak fundamentals. April unwrought aluminium and semi exports rose sharply YoY, while output also increased and consumption remains inhibited by severe COVID conditions. Fed-tightening and recession/sanctions fears further pressured LME base metals. | Brazil’s CBA reported record Q1 EBITDA, attributing the result to higher aluminium prices and premiums. While the piece is largely platform/assessment-related, the key financial linkage highlights recent aluminium price strength, which can bolster expectations for further short-term aluminium pricing.

The piece attributes the move to improved market activity as enterprises resumed work, narrower spot discounts, stronger downstream purchases, and lower secondary aluminium operating rates, outweighing bearish positioning. | SMM reports aluminium scrap throughput at Linyi’s nonferrous metals city rose week-on-week in early May, helped by improved logistics and a small aluminium price increase. However, falling prices and weak downstream alloy demand reduced recycler activity and trading volumes, despite a May 11 rebound in aluminium and taint prices. | Aluminium Bahrain (ALBA) reported Q1 2022 profit of BD146.8m, up 181% YoY, citing higher LME prices driving revenue (LME up 56% YoY) and low LME inventories (~0.6m MT). It also notes demand recovery and improving supply-demand balance, reinforcing positive near-term aluminium sentiment. | Novelis plans a Bay Minette, Alabama, integrated aluminum recycling and rolling plant, positioning it to serve beverage can and automotive markets. The company expects to start commissioning in mid-2025 and discusses future capacity expansion above 600kt, signaling longer-dated aluminum supply and demand changes rather than immediate pricing pressure. | The piece is mainly about dividend-paying gold stocks and a mild rise in gold after US CPI eased rate-hike fears. news_summary: SMM says China’s domestic aluminium billet inventories rose to 138,700 mt as of May 12, up 10,100 mt week-on-week (+7.84%). Smelter restarts in Yunnan boosted shipments to Foshan, while high conversion margins weighed on demand. With easing in east China, firms expect production and orders to recover in May-June, potentially tightening near-term sentiment for aluminium. | SMM says alumina prices are flat domestically but have eased overseas, with FOB Western Australia dropping to about $365/mt. With newly added domestic capacity gradually coming on stream, the market increasingly expects a surplus and further price declines, pending large-scale transaction guidance. | SMM expects bearish near-term aluminium price pressure in May. Marginal power costs provide limited support. | SMM forecasts alumina prices will remain supported as import economics improve (FOB Western Australia decline narrows China–abroad spread) and new capacity ramps in gradually. Pandemic-related transport constraints in northern China disrupted supply, while southwest demand rose with resumed aluminium smelter operations, lifting alumina locally. Prices expected to hold higher-cost support. | The piece highlights that aluminium warehouse stocks are at record lows, reflecting persistently tight physical supply. news_summary: Favourable COVID policies and improving Shanghai conditions boosted market confidence. Falling aluminium ingot social inventories in China’s major markets and a Yunnan smelter cutting production after an accident supported sentiment. SHFE aluminium recorded five straight gain days, with expectations of further short-term rebound, though longer-term upside depends on consumption recovery. | SMM reports China’s domestic alumina prices staying flat at 3,014 yuan/mt as negotiations stall. North China supply increased, while buyers sought significantly lower prices, but sellers resisted due to rising costs. With supply and pricing expectations stuck, trading volumes and the regional weighted index showed no day-on-day change. | Emirates Global Aluminium says it has successfully started up DX+ Ultra reduction cell technology at 500kA, a first for the Middle East. Higher amperage is expected to lift production by about 5% versus current operating limits, supporting potline upgrades and future capacity expansion. | LME data shows aluminium inventories declining steadily since mid-February, reaching 532,500 mt on May 16—the lowest in nearly 17 years. SHFE aluminium inventories also fell 16.33% week-on-week to 246,931 mt as of May 13, the lowest in seven months, signaling tighter supply. | China’s April primary aluminium production rose to 3.36 million mt, a record high, as eased power rationing allowed smelters to expand to full capacity by month-end. Domestic operating capacity is expected to rise in May with plans to resume and new capacity coming online, supporting higher supply despite early-2024 output weakness.

news_summary: Legal issues over land acquisition have been resolved for MIDHANI to set up an aluminium alloy plant at Bodduvaripalem near Nellore with NALCO. The project investment is Rs 2,500–3,000 crore and construction can start. It signals additional aluminium processing demand, mainly a medium-term support factor. | Copper fell 1.1% as markets renewed worries about global slowdown after hawkish Fed signals and weak China data tied to Shanghai COVID lockdown impacts. The stronger dollar pressured dollar-priced metals. LME aluminum and nickel also declined, reflecting broad risk-off sentiment toward industrial metal demand.

news_summary: China’s primary aluminium imports fell sharply in April (down 77.6% YoY) while exports surged (up ~640% YoY). Limited European aluminium supply from energy problems and constrained Rusal exports boosted overseas demand. With import windows loss widening and reduced orders from COVID, tighter flows supported repeated LME aluminium record highs. | SMM says SHFE and LME aluminium are likely to rise this week as social inventories in China keep falling and downstream activity recovers slightly. Lower power costs from Yunnan’s rainy season helped, but an upcoming electricity-demand peak in east and south China may lift coal/energy prices. Overall, aluminium costs are expected to rise short term. | SMM cites International Aluminium Institute (IAI) data showing global primary aluminium production of 5.599 million tonnes in April, with China accounting for about 3.29 million tonnes. North supply improved post-transport recovery, while some bauxite tightness raised costs. | Copper prices climbed to a more than two-week high on a weaker U.S. dollar and improving China demand expectations as lockdown easing and mortgage rate cuts were seen supporting property activity. news_summary: LME copper ended lower on June 7, falling 0.47% to $9,699/mt, as a stronger US dollar reinforced concerns about China’s demand. While LME copper inventories dropped by 20,200 mt to a multiweek low, aluminium inventories fell to a 21-year low, yet aluminium futures still slipped. | On June 8, SHFE aluminium fluctuated around 20,800 yuan/mt and slipped below the daily moving average. The east China aluminium ingot premium stayed around 50 yuan/mt, but warehouse-related issues kept transactions weak. Zhongyuan ingot prices were quoted at discounts of 70–80 yuan/mt to SMM A00, with some at larger discounts. | SMM says aluminium scrap throughput at East China Nonferrous Metal City in Linyi fell sharply in late May/early June and again for the week. The drop is linked to reduced supply as aluminium’s price shock triggered wait-and-see sentiment and some upstream traders took a holiday. Monitoring A00 inventories is urged.

news_summary: Rio Tinto will invest in large-scale wind and solar projects in Queensland to supply electricity for its aluminium operations in the Gladstone region. The company issued a call for proposals to meet energy demand for the Boyne smelter and its Yarwun and Queensland Alumina refineries, replacing coal-fired power. | China’s alumina refineries face higher production costs as bauxite and caustic soda prices jumped in April and northern supply tightened. With alumina prices softening amid oversupply, traders say Henan refineries may trim output, but cuts are uncertain and not yet reflected in prices. Possible aluminium cost/supply effects remain mixed.

news_summary: SMM data show China aluminium production rose in May and more capacity is expected to resume in June, lifting operating capacity and output. Demand remains modest despite easing transport/pandemic issues, with social inventory falling by less than usual. Billet inventory edges higher, implying near-term price pressure for aluminium. | SMM says China’s metallurgical-grade alumina output rose in May, with new capacity driving higher production and shifting fundamentals from shortage to surplus. It notes net imports were negative and alumina prices corrected. More capacity is expected to ramp in Q3, keeping long-term alumina surplus while potentially easing smelter feed costs. | SMM survey data show aluminium billet producers’ operating rates using aluminium liquid rose to 71.2% in May, aided by better aluminium-liquid availability and improving regional supply. North China disruption is expected to improve. Output and downstream demand are forecast to recover in June, lifting operating rates to ~72%. | SMM reports SHFE aluminium rose then fell to close up 0.36% and highlights May’s year-on-year increase in aluminium output. Despite higher production and some capacity gains, social inventories of aluminium ingots kept declining, supporting prices. Net effect is a tug-of-war, implying continued volatility rather than a clear trend.

news_summary: SMM expects SHFE/LME aluminium to remain rangebound. ECB policy signals and US inflation concerns weigh on sentiment, while China’s aluminium supply slightly rises after a capacity restart and output increases. A collateral scandal triggered trader selling and wider spot discounts, but low inventories and possible consumption recovery support prices. | SMM reports China’s metallurgical alumina prices declining across provinces and overseas benchmarks stabilizing around $360/mt FOB WA. Output rose in May and net exports turned to a surplus, shifting the market from deficit to oversupply. Profit erosion from bauxite and caustic costs limits dumping, while smelters’ near-term alumina demand is subdued. | SMM reports domestic alumina prices fell again and Guangxi will apply preferential grid tariffs, potentially lowering electricity costs by about 0.1 yuan/kwh. Aluminium smelter weighted average power costs dropped, reducing smelting costs and expanding profits. In the near term, falling alumina amid excess supply supports softer input costs for aluminium. | Iran’s IMIDRO reported that alumina powder production rose 11% to 42,406 tons in the first two months (March 21–May 21) versus about 38,000 tons a year earlier. Higher alumina supply can support aluminium refining capacity and weigh on aluminium prices short term. | LME aluminium slid to a six-month low as investors feared weaker Chinese demand from COVID lockdowns, higher inflation and interest-rate hikes, and a stronger U.S. dollar. Inventories are at low levels, but macro-driven demand concerns outweighed support. Copper and nickel also declined during the sell-off. | SMM reports China’s social aluminium ingot inventories totalled 825,000 mt on June 13, down from last Thursday and end-May, with declines led by Wuxi, Shanghai and Foshan. Some warehouses/outlets are missing in stats after a collateral scandal, and arrivals may slow destocking. | Aluminum prices weakened in May as China’s Shanghai lockdown returned, curbing manufacturing and reducing aluminum imports, while primary output stayed high. At the same time, LME inventories fell and oil/energy prices rose, supporting costs. Overall direction remains unclear amid competing demand and supply signals.

news_summary: Bauxite mine supply in China tightened sharply, worsening after Indonesia announced a bauxite export ban. Alumina plants in Shanxi, Henan and Guizhou faced shortages and had to buy imported ore at high prices. Rising ore costs and reduced output are expected to keep bauxite prices firm, supporting aluminium via constrained alumina supply. | LME aluminium fell to a six-month low on June 13 amid concern over possible additional China lockdowns and a sell-off linked to rising inflation and interest rates. Demand for industrial metals is expected to weaken. The piece also notes falling LME copper prices, while aluminium inventories are at a multi-year low. | Vietnam’s Ministry of Industry and Trade announced a second review of anti-dumping measures on specified aluminium product imports from China. The review covers several HS-coded items and will reassess the duties’ application, potentially sustaining protection for domestic sellers and influencing aluminium import flows in the near term. | SMM says China’s secondary aluminium alloy plant operating rates rose in May, but prices fell further. Weak auto-related demand, declining die-casting orders, and profit erosion from high scrap and freight costs reduced production and quotes. Import volumes eased as the aluminium price ratio worsened, extending downward pressure. | The Ministry of Industry and Trade announced a second review of anti-dumping measures on specified aluminum product codes imported from China. Initial duties imposed in 2020 and continued after a first review in 2021 remain under assessment, with the investigation citing dumping and harm to the domestic aluminum industry. | American Industrial Partners acquired 100% ownership of the Aluminium Duffel aluminium rolling mill in Belgium from Ecnavla 8 UK Ltd. The deal follows administration and court decisions related to refinancing defaults. AIP expects no operational disruption and plans additional capital, including working capital and investments. | Vietnam’s MoIT announced a second review of anti-dumping duties on specific aluminium product codes imported from China. The original duties (2.49%–35.58%) were imposed after an investigation found dumping and harm to the domestic industry. After the first review, duties were continued, and the second review may keep them in place.

news_summary: Australia faces power shortages after AEMO capped wholesale electricity prices, leading to shutdowns of some coal and gas plants and restricting supply. SMM says aluminium smelters are currently producing normally, but high power costs and spot-market exposure could trigger future production cuts, weighing on aluminium sentiment as LME aluminium falls.

news_summary: Rumours that the same SHFE-approved aluminium ingot batches were repeatedly pledged as collateral triggered panic to ship out cargoes from warehouses in Foshan and other regions. This boosted spot aluminium scarcity and drove large swings, including Gongyi premiums rising from earlier discounts to ~200–280 yuan/mt after the scandal. | China’s May 2022 prebaked anode output rose to 1.88mn mt, with operating rates at 77%. Pre-baked anode prices increased further in June, supported by a petroleum coke rebound. Aluminum smelters are ramping production, boosting anode demand, though carbon profits face pressure from weaker aluminum prices. | Copper prices edged up as traders priced in strike risk at Chile’s Codelco, the world’s largest copper producer, alongside slightly better China activity data. LME copper rose 0.4% on the day, while aluminium weakened on a firmer dollar and softer China prospects; nickel rose modestly. | The Aluminum Extruders Council urged the Biden Administration to defend CBP’s EAPA findings that Kingtom avoided U.S. aluminum extrusion duties by transshipping Chinese-origin extrusions via the Dominican Republic. The group also seeks CBP action to halt imports tied to forced-labor violations, potentially tightening supply and supporting aluminum prices.

news_summary: SMM says domestic alumina prices were quiet but slightly down, with spot prices falling on abundant supply after new capacity commissioning and worsened cash flows leading traders/refineries to dump cargoes. Overseas prices rose moderately as smelters increased purchases to keep production stable. Some refinery cuts slowed declines, but an outright rebound depends on costs vs. prices. | SMM reports that a 75 bp US Fed hike and additional rate increases abroad have pressured commodities, including SHFE and LME aluminium. Domestically, supply growth is accelerating, with some smelters turning loss-making and traders shifting/cancelling orders, limiting support despite declining ingot inventories. | SMM says alumina prices in China fell but the decline slowed as overseas buying picked up, tightening supply and lifting FOB Western Australia prices. Domestic pressure eased where cash-flow stress forced some refineries to cut output. Smelters relied on long-term supply, limiting spot demand, but costs remain high. | SMM reports a slight rise in China’s domestic bauxite prices due to tighter bauxite availability for alumina refineries. Guinea’s military government asked bauxite miners to submit alumina-refinery construction timetables, considers a bauxite reference price, and raises export uncertainty, potentially lifting imported bauxite costs and widening refinery cost disparities. | SMM reports alumina costs edged lower while thermal coal was slightly firmer, leaving electricity prices largely unchanged for aluminium smelters. Despite anode prices staying high, smelters’ average costs fell but profits dropped sharply as aluminium spot prices plunged. Raw material costs may fall further, though electricity-driven coal strength could keep costs elevated. | China’s aluminium ingot social inventories in eight major markets were 757,000 mt as of June 20, down 28,000 mt week-on-week and 165,000 mt from end-May. The decline was led by Wuxi and Foshan, aided by cargo transfers and stronger downstream purchases after price pullbacks, with limited arrivals helping inventories fall.

news_summary: Alcoa marked the 120th anniversary of its Massena, New York aluminum smelter, noting 450 employees and long-term production for transportation, construction, defense, and aerospace. The facility is described as the oldest continuously operating aluminum smelter and recently earned ASI certifications, with an emphasis on ESG performance rather than output changes. | SMM reports China’s primary aluminium ingot imports fell 61% YoY in May while exports surged, leaving small net imports. The SHFE/LME ratio rose in June as domestic prices held up versus LME, narrowing import losses and suggesting the import window may reopen, while weaker overseas orders may reduce semis exports. | Japanese firms are reportedly substituting away from copper as copper prices surge. Daikin plans to cut copper usage by about 60% in 2023, and Panasonic is considering air-conditioner models using aluminium instead of copper for core components. The shift may dampen copper demand and mildly lift aluminium demand. | SMM reports SHFE aluminium falling 0.84% to a six-month low amid bearish macro sentiment after multiple central banks’ rate hikes and recession fears, with expectations of further US hikes. Energy supply concerns in Europe and power price pressure add headwinds. Social inventories declined, but rising supply and weak demand cap rebounds. | SMM reports IAI data showing global primary aluminium production (excluding China) rebounded to 2.21 million tonnes in May. The daily average still slipped to 713,900 tonnes, the lowest in about 1.5 years, signaling supply remained constrained but improved versus the trough.

news_summary: China’s bauxite imports hit a record high in May, rising sharply year-on-year, with large volumes from Guinea and Indonesia. Indonesia export-ban rumors and Guinea regime instability could restrict supply and lift imported bauxite prices. Higher feedstock costs are expected to pressure alumina costs and support aluminium prices. | SMM reports China’s primary aluminium imports in May 2022 totaled about 37,305 mt, up 6.96% month-on-month but down 61% year-on-year. Imports from January to May fell to 168,700 mt, down 71% YoY, indicating significantly reduced primary aluminium inflows.

news_summary: Century Aluminum will shut down its Hawesville, Kentucky smelter, cutting over 600 jobs. The company cites energy costs nearly tripling due to the war in Ukraine. The closure is expected to last 9–12 months and comes after prior temporary shutdowns blamed on Chinese overcapacity, implying reduced near-term aluminum supply. | SMM reports sharp declines in SHFE and LME aluminium prices, citing US rate-hike expectations and weaker manufacturing data as bearish for demand. Despite lower Chinese aluminium ingot and billet inventories, spot transactions stay weak. Rising domestic operating capacity and overall macro tightening are expected to keep aluminium prices pressured in the near term. | SMM reports China’s aluminium ingot social inventories fell to 751,000 mt by June 23, down 172,000 mt in a month, driven by sharply lower imports, increased exports of ingots and semis, more smelting output as aluminium liquid, and dip buying by downstream users. Smelters also reduced warehouse shipments after a collateral scandal, aiding destocking. | SMM’s survey finds aluminium plate/sheet and strip demand remains subdued. Many Chinese producers report new orders falling by more than 50% year-on-year, while both domestic sales and foreign trade are sluggish. This indicates weaker near-term aluminium consumption and potential downward pressure on prices. | Century Aluminum plans to temporarily halt its Hawesville, Kentucky smelter for nine to 12 months starting in August, laying off about 600 workers. The company cites dramatically higher power costs. The Hawesville plant is its largest U.S. smelter and a major high-purity primary aluminum producer.

news_summary: SHFE aluminium fell 4.22% on the week and LME was also down, reflecting bearish macro pressure from Fed/ECB rate-hike expectations. Fundamentals are soft: domestic aluminium capacity is set to rise, but processing sectors receive limited orders and July-August is an off-season. Inventories are low, limiting downside. | SMM reports alumina prices roughly flat week-on-week, with most Chinese regions holding within set ranges. Some Shanxi/Henan losses from higher caustic soda and imported bauxite prompted production cuts, while ore-related outages are gradually resuming elsewhere. Tight balance from stable exports and returning aluminium output underpins current prices, despite long-run surplus risk. | SMM reports aluminium smelters’ profits shrinking sharply as spot aluminium prices fell to a new year low (19,160 yuan/mt as of June 24). Despite a drop in smelting costs, bearish aluminium market factors and potentially sticky coal costs near peak power demand could push aluminium prices lower and risk losses. | ALRO’s Vimetco Extrusion installed and tested a new automated extrusion press line after a ~€10m investment. The 7” line aims to produce higher value aluminium profiles with lower energy and gas use, adding 11,000 tonnes of annual capacity to reach 35,000 tonnes by end-2022. | China’s eight major markets recorded aluminium ingot social inventories of 746,000 mt as of June 27, down slightly on the week but sharply less than end-May. Destocking slowed materially. Wuxi outflows fell as collateral-scan-dal effects eased, while Gongyi inventory rose on arrivals and weak downstream buying. | Codelco says Chile’s tightening environmental and water regulations will force strategy changes, including recirculating water, cutting emissions, shifting to renewables, and previously closing its Ventanas smelter. The drought is still affecting output, while permitting delays and potential glacier rules add uncertainty for future projects. | ALRO’s downstream unit Vimetco Extrusion has completed a new automatic press extrusion line for aluminium profiles, following nearly EUR10m investment since March 2021. The added extrusion capacity may increase near-term demand for aluminium billets/feedstock, supporting expectations for aluminium consumption in the period.

news_summary: SMM reports SHFE aluminium moving in a broad range amid recession worries but notes cost support and supply tightening. Alcoa’s Hawesville smelter and Century’s Kentucky plant closure were cited, alongside falling LME and domestic inventories. The piece expects SHFE aluminium to trade between 18,900-19,600 yuan/mt near term. | China’s policy briefings and additional measures to stabilize the economy are portrayed as turning the macro outlook more optimistic, lifting non-ferrous prices. SHFE aluminium is reported up 2.34% and spot A00 prices higher, with firmer seller pricing/cost support and some rebound after shorts covered. | Western Copper and Gold reported positive feasibility results for its Casino copper-gold-molybdenum project, including project economics that depend on assumed base-case copper prices. news_summary: Romanian alumina producer Alum Tulcea plans to suspend production starting in August, according to its activity plan. Because alumina is a key feedstock for aluminium smelting, the shutdown raises concerns about tighter supply of refining inputs, which can pressure expectations for near-term aluminium prices.

news_summary: LME copper stayed near a 16-month low as investors weighed hawkish central bank signals and potential rapid rate hikes that could hurt growth and demand. LME nickel futures rose after UK sanctions involving Norilsk Nickel’s major shareholder. Aluminium premiums for July–September were agreed lower, suggesting softer near-term aluminium pricing. | Rising European energy costs, intensified by geopolitical tensions and strikes (including at TotalEnergies), are squeezing aluminium smelter margins and raising expectations of further curtailments. Traders say losses exceed profitability thresholds, limiting supply and supporting Rotterdam aluminium premiums despite declines in the LME aluminium cash price.

news_summary: China Molybdenum plans to invest over $1.8 billion in the Kisanfu/KFM copper-cobalt project in the DRC, including spending on engineering and construction. The mine is expected to supply about 90,000 tons of cathode copper annually after full output, starting production in the first half of next year. | SMM reports a surge in newly commissioned Chinese alumina capacity, pushing alumina prices into a downward trend since May 16. Inland, high-cost refineries in Shanxi and Henan face bauxite shortages and reduced profits, cutting operating rates or shutting furnaces. Falling alumina and ongoing supply tightness may ease smelter costs. | SMM reports that an aluminium rally proved temporary: SHFE aluminium fell 1.42% after improving but still mixed macro data. Weak end demand ahead of the off-season and slower destocking coincide with higher domestic operating capacity (up to ~41mn mt) and only modest inventory declines, weighing on prices. | London copper dropped as much as 3.6% to around $7,959/ton, extending a bear market and marking the lowest level since early 2021. It fell nearly 20% in the quarter amid recession and growth fears, with similar weakness cited for aluminium and nickel.

news_summary: The piece describes intensified Western Australia oversight of Alcoa’s bauxite mining, including an unprecedented $55 million forest-clearing fine and ongoing investigations over approvals and exemption conditions. It also notes regulator actions that could add stricter constraints, with knock-on concern for alumina output, affecting aluminium supply expectations. | Alcoa said it will begin immediately curtailing one of three smelting lines at its Warrick, Indiana aluminium smelter because of operational challenges. Each line is about 54,000 mt annual capacity. Alcoa plans to protect output from the remaining two lines while reducing total capacity safely. | SMM reports alumina prices are largely stable week-on-week as high-cost refineries in north China cut output due to bauxite shortages and shrinking margins. Regional prices are steady and overseas alumina transactions fell, suggesting falling CIF levels. Aluminium smelters find prices acceptable, so near-term aluminium impact looks limited. | SMM says China’s eight major markets’ aluminium ingot social inventories were 734,000 mt on July 4, flat from last Thursday, indicating the destocking cycle has paused. Foshan and Gongyi rose (Gongyi above 100,000 mt for four straight weeks). Spot trading softened as the off-season nears, and inventories may edge higher. | SHFE aluminium fell below 19,000 yuan/mt amid bearish macro signals from Powell and expected ECB rate hikes. Meanwhile China’s aluminium supply expanded with new capacity coming online. Downstream demand weakened (construction, auto, and wire/cable orders), inventories destocking slowed, and the market risk shifts toward surplus, pressuring prices further. | A StrategyR/GIA market study projects the global aluminum composite panels market to grow to about $10B by 2026 from $7.1B in 2020, with steady growth by segment and region. While it suggests rising end-demand for aluminium inputs, it contains no short-term supply or pricing triggers. | CMOC says its KFM copper-cobalt mine in the DRC will start producing in 1H23, targeting average output of about 90,000 tpy copper metal (and 30,000 tpy cobalt) at full production. The company argues the additional cobalt supply helps EV demand, but near-term price effects are limited. | Japan-based aluminium buyers are set to pay lower premiums for July–September imports. Falling import premiums generally indicate less tightness in the physical market and/or softer demand conditions, which can weigh on aluminium price expectations in the near term by improving availability and reducing buyer urgency.

news_summary: Credit Suisse’s recession scenarios point to downside for copper and aluminium: copper could drop for longer amid incoming supply, while aluminium faces negative demand pressure despite possible support from constrained supply and higher energy/input costs. It also discusses broader iron ore resilience and a bauxite outlook that is mainly longer-term. | Credit Suisse analysis flags recession scenarios as negative for copper and aluminium: copper could fall longer due to incoming supply, while aluminium faces demand weakness though it may get support from constrained supply and higher energy/input costs. news_summary: China’s bauxite imports in May reached 11.97 Mt, a new record, led by Guinea and also higher Australia/Indonesia-linked volumes. With Indonesia repeatedly rumored to ban exports and Guinea’s regime instability, supply uncertainty persists. Strong refinery demand is expected to push up bauxite costs and support aluminium prices. | SMM reports alumina prices have bottomed out as high-cost refineries cut output amid losses. In northern China, supply tightens and local spot prices hold firm, while the south-west faces renewed buyer-favourable pressure from new capacity. Overall, alumina downside is limited in the short run due to curtailments. | SMM reports aluminium prices falling sharply as domestic supply rises with resumed production and new capacity, while demand weakens due to July seasonality, high southern temperatures, and sluggish real estate. Inventory declines are slowing, and overseas semi exports growth may fade. SMM expects SHFE aluminium to drop further in July-August. | SMM says China’s molten aluminium casting billet operating rates fell to 72.4% in June, with weaker terminal consumption and lower order volumes in several regions. Billet social inventory rose amid supply-demand tension, conversion margins were pressured, and conditions are expected to deteriorate in the July off-season. | Copper prices fell to a 20-month low as economic slowdown fears intensified. A stronger U.S. dollar, renewed COVID-19 curbs in China, and expectations of aggressive U.S. rate hikes ahead of inflation data reduced demand outlook. China’s growth cuts and market caution before China data reinforced selling pressure. Aluminium also fell; nickel was mixed. | Proud Source Water, Ball Corporation, and Replenysh launched a Florida pilot to improve aluminum recycling infrastructure, targeting recovery of at least six million pounds of aluminum over 12 months. The program places schools as collection “host locations” to divert aluminum cans from landfill, increasing recycled supply.

news_summary: SMM reports secondary aluminium operating rates rose 4.3% in June to 43%, supported by easing pandemic effects and improving auto supply chains. However, low new order volumes and weak die-casting demand left downstream purchasing cautious. Inventories increased and firms cut prices, suggesting operating rates may fall in July. | SMM data show China’s aluminium ingot social inventories fell to 697,000 mt as of July 14, driven by downstream dip buying, reduced warehouse arrivals due to pandemic impacts, and active cargo outflows. Billet inventories also eased overall, though arrivals rose in some regions. Lower prices boosted purchasing sentiment.

news_summary: London and Shanghai copper prices are set for their biggest weekly loss in over two years, pressured by a stronger US dollar and weak demand outlook tied to slowing growth and imminent rate hikes. China’s COVID-hit activity also weighs. Nickel sharply falls too; aluminium edges up slightly. | Vedanta Aluminium announced on World Youth Skills Day it is expanding training and placement-linked skill programmes for rural youth in Odisha and other states. It highlights work near its alumina refinery and aluminium smelter operations, including farmers’ training and rural women’s cooperatives, but no direct production, pricing, or trade changes are cited. | S&P Global says copper is the bottleneck for 2050 net-zero, projecting demand rising sharply and supply failing to meet it due to long mine lead times and constraints on expanding production and recycling. With limited substitution—including insufficient aluminium and recycling—near-term expectations for tight copper supply could support higher prices.

news_summary: SHFE 2208 and LME aluminium prices extended declines, with contracts down about 6.6% on the week. The report attributes the fall to bearish macro—US CPI and higher rate-hike odds—and aluminium fundamentals: many smelters are loss-making, downstream orders remain weak, and destocking slows, keeping prices pressured. | SMM reports China’s aluminium ingot social inventories at 682,000 mt as of July 18, down from last Thursday and significantly lower than a year earlier. Shipments from warehouses increased, and downstream buyers became more willing to purchase after prices dipped near 17,000 yuan/mt. Arrivals in Wuxi should normalize after pandemic disruption. | SMM reports that aluminium smelters face higher power costs as coal supply tightens during peak summer electricity demand. Electricity prices rose 1.6% to 0.439 yuan/kwh, pushing real-time smelter costs up 58.6 yuan/mt. Meanwhile, A00 aluminium prices fell 5.5%, expanding smelter losses and risking further short-term deterioration. | SMM reports alumina prices diverging across China as ocean FOB Western Australia prices fall on abundant supply, while north China gets partial refinery recoveries but bauxite costs constrain output. South-west prices weaken with new capacity releases. Record aluminium smelter operating rates suggest only a slight alumina surplus, limiting sharp aluminium moves. | Copper fell 7.8% over the week to $3.2 per pound, its worst rate since November two years ago. Nickel declined 11% and aluminium dropped 3% (zinc also down). The move is linked to recession fears and wider commodity weakness, alongside European gas supply concerns from Gazprom. | Copper jumped 3.3% on LME after investors covered shorts following Chinese government support for the property sector and signs of monetary easing, helped by a weaker dollar. CMOC’s Tenke Fungurume suspended exports. Aluminium rose 3.8% and nickel gained 6.5%, reflecting broad industrial metals strength.

news_summary: SMM said Shanghai aluminium ingot social inventory was 671,000 mt, up on the week, while billet inventory rose to 107,600 mt. Some regions saw increases due to intensive cargo arrivals and sluggish downstream demand. Spot transactions were weak, and inventories are unlikely to fall near term. | Ellwood Group said it will invest in its Ellwood Specialty Steel plant to add an aluminum tooling plate (ATP) production line. The automated facility includes sawing, machining, quality control, lamination, storage and packaging, aiming to supply customers more reliably with reduced lead times and inventory uncertainty. | Hydro Aluminum plans a $15 million investment in its Henderson, Kentucky recycled-aluminum facility, including a new homogenization furnace and a baghouse dust/air system. The upgrades aim to boost output of certified low-carbon aluminum and enable processing of higher scrap volumes, supporting production for the automotive market.

Three-month LME copper gained 0.8% and Shanghai September copper rose 1.3% amid ongoing demand worries. The article also reports gains in LME aluminium and ShFE nickel, suggesting broadly positive metal sentiment. | SMM reports China’s aluminium wheel exports fell in June to 71,500 mt, down 6% month-on-month and 10% year-on-year. LME aluminum is up modestly, indicating weaker bearish spillover to aluminium. | The piece forecasts steady expansion of the global aluminum cans market (notably in North America and China) driven by rising beverage and processed-food consumption and sustainability/recyclability trends. It also notes manufacturers focusing on increasing production capacity to meet demand, implying supportive near-term aluminum demand.

news_summary: SMM reports regional alumina price divergence in China, with north China supported by higher costs and expected Shanxi production cuts for environmental reasons. FOB Western Australia prices eased slightly as overseas supply improved. South-west prices remain firm but may soften with future output releases. Alumina is seen fluctuating narrowly this week. | China’s bauxite imports fell to 9.42 million mt in June, down 21.4% MoM, with Australia and Indonesia both declining sharply. Weather disruptions hurt shipments, and Indonesia’s export ban risk has intensified, prompting Chinese alumina refineries to seek alternative ores. Supply tightness could support aluminium pricing. | SMM reports China exported 340,800 mt of aluminium plate/sheet in June 2022, down 6% month-on-month but up 44% year-on-year. Exports weakened as overseas fabrication recovery did not translate into sustained orders, and SMM expects continued MoM export declines in coming months, pressuring aluminium demand expectations. | SMM said China’s aluminium ingot social inventories totaled 678,000 mt on Aug 1, up 7,000 mt week-on-week but below last year. Foshan inventories rose for two weeks amid weak trades and steady arrivals. With low arrivals elsewhere, demand remains poor, and inventories may increase further as shipments arrive. | Koba Resources completed induced polarisation (IP) surveys over its historic Blackpine cobalt-copper project ahead of maiden drilling in September, following an earlier maiden program at the nearby Colson project. The work targets extensions of copper-cobalt mineralisation, but no production changes are reported.

news_summary: Aluminium prices fell sharply on both SHFE and LME after the prior rally, with spot discounts widening. SMM links the weakness to accumulating domestic social inventories, poor downstream demand and lower operating rates, and smelter production pressures from loss-making conditions. High energy costs provide some support, but upside momentum appears limited.

news_summary: Hydro Aluminum Metals USA will upgrade and expand its Henderson, Kentucky plant with a new homogenization furnace and baghouse to improve automotive specs and increase recycling of broader post-consumer scrap. Work starts August and finishes by September 2024, supported by Kentucky tax incentives tied to investment and job retention. | Copper prices eased as U.S.-China tensions over Pelosi’s Taiwan visit raised fears of weaker industrial demand. Markets also discounted the prospect of more Fed rate hikes and recession risks. LME three-month copper fell 1.6%, with aluminium down 1.3% and nickel down 0.8%, reflecting broad metals pressure.

news_summary: SMM expects aluminium prices in August to fluctuate but remain pressured by weak downstream demand in an off-season, falling profit-driven reluctance to sell/produce by high-cost smelters, and inventory accumulation risks for ingot and billet. Costs may ease via lower electricity and anode prices, but demand is slow to improve. | SMM reports August aluminium fluoride tender prices were cut to 8,500 yuan/mt, with weaker cost support from falling sulphuric acid prices. Lower bidding by aluminium plants reduced aluminium fluoride producers’ profits, prompting some to switch to hydrofluoric acid. SMM expects aluminium fluoride prices to face short-term downside and muted transactions. | AMAG and Audi developed a new recycled aluminium casting alloy (AlSi7.REC) for car wheels with over 70% recycled content, aiming to meet safety and aesthetic requirements while cutting CO2 versus primary metal. The piece also cites recent Fastmarkets aluminium pricing levels, but provides no large-scale volume impact. | Murchison Minerals says it has commenced Phase III diamond drilling at its HPM Quebec nickel-copper-cobalt project, targeting expansion and delineation at the Barre de Fer zone and the nearby Syrah target. The company plans ~7,500 m and references assay grades, but no production or supply changes are announced.

news_summary: Customs data cited by SMM shows China exported about 652,200 mt of unwrought aluminium and aluminium semis in July 2022, down 5.01% month-on-month but up 39.05% year-on-year. January–July exports rose 34.9% YoY to 4.16 million mt, indicating weaker near-term outward shipments. | SMM reports SHFE and LME aluminium ended the week lower/flat-to-higher but with weak momentum. Europe’s energy/power crisis and poor profitability slow smelter restarts and raise expectations for cuts, while weak downstream demand and rising ingot inventories widen spot discounts. Net effect is rangebound price action. | SMM reports that aluminium smelter costs fell after rounds of cuts to prebaked anode and aluminium fluoride prices. Despite stable power tariffs for most firms, the average smelter cost dropped to 17,884 yuan/mt and profits rose to 635 yuan/mt as A00 aluminium prices increased to 18,520 yuan/mt. Risks remain from price volatility. | SMM said Chinese aluminium ingot social inventories totaled 687,000 mt as of Aug 8, up for three consecutive weeks (+17,000 mt since early August) and higher than end-July. Several regions saw stock increases, while warehouse shipments fell to 128,300 mt and spot discounts widened amid sluggish demand.

news_summary: German KfW IPEX-Bank reiterated full support for Altech Chemicals’ high-purity alumina (HPA) project in Malaysia, including a planned US$190 million senior loan, with Euler Hermes renewing export credit cover. The update follows discussions on financing and bankable progress, enabling work to resume after pandemic-related delays. | State Grid announced it will start multiple Ultra High Voltage transmission projects and accelerate additional projects, investing over 150 billion yuan. UHV lines in China use aluminium bare overhead conductor (ACSR), implying substantial aluminum rod demand (SMM estimates 700,000 mt+ over construction). Analysts expect supportive effects on aluminium demand and prices. | A German aluminium foundry is preparing for temporary shutdowns to survive Europe’s gas crisis, with gas costs more than doubling and likely tripling. Aluminium smelters/foundries depend heavily on natural gas and may reduce output, raising concerns about downstream delivery disruptions across auto supply chains. | SMM says China produced 3.5m tonnes of aluminium in July, up 6.7% y/y, with domestic operating capacity continuing to rise. August output is expected to increase further, while demand for sheets/plate, strip and extrusion weakens. Inventories of ingots and rod may keep rising, weighing on aluminium prices. | A German aluminium foundry plans rotating shutdowns to survive Europe’s gas crisis as energy costs have doubled and gas prices have surged on reduced Nord Stream 1 flows. news_summary: Sichuan expanded emergency industrial electricity cuts due to extreme heat and drought lowering hydropower. SMM flags potential future supply gaps, supporting aluminium price strength. | Hydro’s Slovak Slovalco smelter will close its primary aluminium production, citing high electricity prices and an uncompetitive CO2 compensation framework. Operations will end by late September 2022 after prior curtailments. Recycling continues at 75,000 tpy, but primary supply loss is supportive for aluminium prices. | Norsk Hydro is shutting an aluminum plant as Europe’s power woes deepen. The closure reduces aluminum supply from a major producer, worsening short-term availability. With electricity costs cited as the driver, markets may expect more curtailments, supporting aluminium prices in the near term. | SHFE and LME aluminium prices rose after news of production cuts: Hydro will shut Slovakia’s Slovalco primary aluminium plant and Sunndal strike begins Aug 22, while Sichuan power rationing has reduced provincial aluminium output. The supply outlook tightened, lifting SHFE aluminium by 3.75% and LME by 1.30% despite mixed demand signals. | Reuters reports Shanghai aluminium prices rallied on supply concerns tied to European and China production disruptions. Norsk Hydro will close a Slovak primary aluminium plant due to high electricity prices, and China’s heatwave power controls disrupted aluminium production. ShFE aluminium rose sharply, with nickel also edging higher.

news_summary: Biden’s federal climate and health legislation includes tax credits and grants aimed at lowering the cost of producing low-carbon aluminum in the U.S. It may help the Intalco Works smelter’s reopening in Washington, though the timing and ultimate restart remain uncertain. Overall support is likely for near-term aluminium sentiment.

news_summary: SMM reports LME aluminium inventories dropped by 2,350 mt on August 18 to a new record low. Most of the decline came from the approved warehouse in Klang (-1,500 mt), with additional falls in Kaohsiung (-625 mt), Baltimore (-125 mt), and Bilbao (-100 mt). | Norsk Hydro will stop primary output at its Slovalco aluminium smelter by end-September due to high electricity prices, while possible strikes at Hydro and Alcoa Norwegian plants could cut additional production. With falling LME warehouse stocks and stressed logistics, European aluminium premiums are pressured but supply tightness is seen as supportive.

news_summary: SMM cites China customs data showing primary aluminium imports of 51,037.4 mt in July, down 82.1% MoM and 97.2% YoY. Exports reached 7,588.4 mt, up 14.6% MoM and 16x YoY, with much higher export totals from January to July. | Alumina Limited’s managing director says the inflationary pressures that raised its unit costs by 32% over the past year have likely peaked. The firm notes alumina prices have fallen from an average of about $398/tonne over six months to roughly $330/tonne recently, easing cost pressures and supporting the aluminium value chain. | BNN Bloomberg notes that an aluminium rally has helped Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) achieve record profits. This suggests favourable aluminium price levels and/or demand conditions for the producer in the near term, supporting market expectations for aluminium. | Wildpack Beverage announced a long-term purchase and supply agreement with Ball Corporation for sustainable aluminum beverage packaging. The deal expands Wildpack’s printed can and warehousing services across its U.S. locations, supporting ongoing demand for aluminum packaging and potentially improving near-term sentiment for aluminium prices. | A report says a German aluminum smelter is considering halving production because energy costs are too high. If implemented, lower primary aluminum output could affect regional supply expectations and market pricing in the near term, depending on how quickly alternative production or inventories can offset the reduction.

news_summary: China’s unwrought aluminium alloy imports fell 13% MoM in July but rose strongly YoY, while exports surged nearly 50% MoM. Import losses were easing as aluminium prices rallied since mid-July. An opened import window in early August pushed Malaysia ADC12 quotes up, despite losses widening to 300–400 yuan/mt. | Speira is considering reducing output at its German aluminium smelter to about half to cope with soaring electricity and natural-gas costs, deciding in September. With many European smelters already curtailing, the risk of tighter regional aluminium supply is rising, which supports near-term LME aluminium price strength. | Aluminium markets are pressured by Sichuan power rationing that is pushing more Chinese smelters to cut or suspend output, while overseas producers face strikes (Hydro Sunndal, Alcoa Mosjoen) and energy-price-driven reductions. Despite some eased supply, weak downstream demand limits upside, keeping prices volatile but supported by supply tightness. | S&P Global/IHS Markit research warns a copper supply gap will open by 2025, with demand surging from electrification and recycling/substitution not closing the shortfall. It argues higher prices are the likely market response to persistent deficits, potentially constraining the net-zero energy transition. Aluminium substitution is limited to about 1% of demand. | Aluminium prices saw a modest rise as smelter closures and temporary shutdowns—driven by high energy costs in Europe and drought/heatwave in China—tightened supply. However, Reuters notes worsening macro data, high rates and inflation are weakening demand, likely capping further upside despite falling LME inventories. | Monster says port congestion and high freight costs have pushed it to continue importing aluminum cans to avoid stockouts, despite aiming to shift more sourcing to U.S. suppliers. The company incurred higher aluminum can costs in Q1 and Q2 and expects cost-of-sales relief via more domestic can usage, while raising prices Sept. 1.

The company also highlighted moves to low-carbon metals, adding copper exposure via Sierra Gorda and expanding aluminium capacity through Mozal and a restarted Brazil smelter. news_summary: SMM says aluminium scrap throughput at Linyi Huadong rose sharply to about 15,300 mt in Aug 22-28. Domestic A00 aluminium prices increased to 18,860 yuan/mt and added another 100 yuan to 18,960. Scrap recyclers sold more amid weak momentum, but downstream demand—especially for tense scrap—remains strong. | CRISIL Research says aluminium prices have likely bottomed and are set to rise as a global deficit looms. It attributes the rebound to limited additions to smelter capacity and a pickup in demand, which should tighten supply and support higher aluminium prices over the coming weeks. | Construction is underway for a new Hydro Aluminum plant in Cassopolis. The facility will use raw aluminum and recycled scrap to produce aluminum bars, targeting demand including the automotive sector. Hydro says the project will create about 100 jobs and expects operations to start in fall 2023, implying limited immediate pricing impact. | Fastmarkets reports that aluminium extrusion billet premiums in the US and Europe declined as demand weakened and supply stayed ample. Underlying primary aluminium premiums eased too, while producers faced higher energy costs that compressed margins. Thin spot activity and cautious buying further pressured premiums across multiple regions. | Fastmarkets’ preview highlights ongoing European aluminium smelter cuts from high energy costs and Ukraine-related disruption, while demand remains uncertain and consumers are not buying against full order books. Aluminium premiums have fallen from record highs, and LME stocks are low, with macro recession fears adding bearish sentiment toward near-term prices.

news_summary: Rio Tinto plans a new aluminum recycling plant in Saguenay-Lac-Saint-Jean, Quebec, aiming to remelt locally sourced post-consumer aluminum scrap into billets at the Arvida smelter. The facility is expected to start in Q2 2024 with 30,000 tons/year capacity, boosting low-carbon recycled supply.

news_summary: SMM reports a Shandong alumina refinery shut one 900,000 mt/year alumina line after an accident, removing an estimated 70,000–75,000 mt of alumina output. Production is expected to resume mid-to-late September. The temporary alumina supply tightening is likely supportive for aluminium prices over the next several weeks. | SMM said China’s social aluminium ingot inventories rose to 683,000 mt (+4,000 mt WoW) but remain lower vs a year ago, led by gains in Wuxi and transfers from Foshan. Billet inventories slightly fell (-500 mt WoW). Demand is weak, and Sichuan smelter output remains constrained, limiting further accumulation. | MacDonald Mines reported Phase 1 drill results at its SPJ property, including a 0.41% CuEq (with copper values and associated gold/silver) over 90.44m at the Alwyn trend and cobalt anomalies. news_summary: SMM says Yunnan’s aluminium smelters are mostly operating normally, but hydropower shortages and lower reservoir water raise concerns of power rationing and output curtailment after the dry season. Yunnan’s growing smelter capacity could be cut if 20–30% of capacity is curtailed, easing domestic supply pressure. | Copper prices touched a one-month low earlier amid China’s extended COVID restrictions in Shenzhen and a lockdown in Chengdu, raising recession and demand concerns; potential smelter shutdowns could reduce supply. Risk sentiment improved after U.S. jobs data eased Fed hike fears. Aluminium support came from Aldel mothballing capacity due to energy costs.

news_summary: SMM reports alumina prices were slightly lower week-on-week amid mixed supply moves: some Chongqing and other plants resumed after power restoration, while others cut output due to accidents, ongoing bauxite shortages, or delayed availability. Aluminium smelters are restarting in Sichuan, but Yunnan hydropower concerns may curb output and raise risks for alumina demand. | SMM reports SHFE and LME aluminium prices fell on the week amid hawkish rate expectations and stronger USD, plus weak macro demand signals. While Sichuan power is restored, Yunnan reservoir declines may trigger smelter load cuts and supply uncertainty. Overall, aluminium is expected to remain volatile with support and downside risks balancing. | Shanghai (SMM) said China’s aluminium ingot social inventories in eight major markets fell to 681,000 mt on Sep 5, down slightly week-on-week and more than last year. Regional moves were small. Despite Sichuan smelter shutdowns in August, inventory didn’t drop sharply, but downstream purchases improved after price declines, supporting demand. | European aluminium producers face potential smelter shutdowns as an energy crisis—linked to Gazprom supply cut-offs and soaring electricity costs—makes production uneconomic. Norsk Hydro’s Slovak plant is at risk, with European output at the lowest since the 1970s and analysts expecting large capacity closures, some permanent. | SMM reports weaker downstream appetite for aluminium scrap in Linyi, with aluminium scrap throughput falling sharply week-on-week due to reluctance to buy after September stocking. Aluminium scrap prices and overall aluminium prices dropped last week, reflecting bearish sentiment and inventory dynamics.

The shutdown begins next Monday and should finish before October 1, amid broader European smelter curtailments and uncertain restart timing without losses. | China’s August unwrought copper and copper product imports rose year-on-year as July-August price declines prompted producer buying and arbitrage between Shanghai and London increased inflows. Analysts link the shift to lessons from disruption after earlier sanctions, suggesting increased supply into Western markets. | A report says a German aluminum smelter has cut production by half because power costs have surged. The output reduction points to immediate curtailments in primary aluminum supply, which can tighten near-term availability and likely provide upward pressure on aluminium prices over the next several weeks. | Boeing said it stopped buying aluminum from Russia in March after suspending operations in the sanctions-hit country. It follows an earlier pause on Russian titanium sourcing. Boeing cited widespread aluminum sourcing but the move, alongside Russia’s major role via Rusal, may tighten near-term supply expectations for aluminium. | Irish regulator approved Aughinish Alumina’s plan to expand by-product storage at its Askeaton alumina refinery, raising red mud pond and salt cake storage heights. The expansion supports deposition of about one million cubic metres per annum and helps avoid a projected 2030 operational stop, supporting alumina supply continuity. | Brazil’s alumina exports rose 62% year-on-year in August to 908,008 tonnes, with higher shipments via São Luís and Belém. news_summary: Europe’s energy crisis is forcing aluminium smelters to cut output, highlighted by Dunkirk Aluminium’s 22% production reduction. Soaring electricity costs make production loss-making and lead to broader curtailments. The article notes domestic aluminium prices rose nearly 10% since mid-July, supporting bullish price pressure. | SMM reports that Indonesia may halt bauxite exports, alongside earlier nickel ore export restrictions. Chinese alumina refineries relying on Indonesian ore are considering alternatives such as Guinea, Australia, Montenegro, Jamaica, and other sources. The move raises near-term expectations of tighter bauxite/alumina supply for China. | Europe faces soaring electricity costs after Russia’s Nord Stream 1 suspension, with natural gas power a key input to generation. European aluminium smelters are already cutting output—over 1 million mt curtailed—and additional cuts are expected before winter. Dunkirk, Alcoa Norway and Hydro plan sizable reductions, temporarily supporting prices. | Fastmarkets reports aluminium P1020A premia weakening across key regions. Japan’s MJP premium fell as traders noted low deliverability and heavy inventories. Europe and Brazil premiums continued downtrends amid weak spot demand and ample availability, despite reported energy-related smelter curtailments. US premia were flat but at very low levels. | York Harbour Metals reports preliminary assays from Phase 3 drilling at its Newfoundland York Harbour Copper-Zinc Project, including a drill interval with 2.86% copper over 14.77 metres. Management says the discovery expands the D Zone and could extend along strike and downdip. Results for additional core are pending. | Boeing has stopped buying Russian aluminum again, ending sourcing of a “crucial metal” from Russia for the second time since the Ukraine invasion. The move reduces demand for Russian aluminium supply, potentially tightening near-term availability and supporting aluminium prices as buyers shift away from Russia. | The piece argues aluminium prices remain volatile as energy costs and inflation force many smelters in Europe and the USA to curtail or shut down due to uneconomic production. It examines scrap and secondary aluminium trends, noting recycling can cut energy use and emissions, but depends on local, sufficient scrap availability. | Speira will curtail 50% of aluminium smelter production at Rheinwerk, Germany, cutting output to 70,000 tonnes/year from October through November, citing high European energy costs. The curtailed output will be replaced via external supplies. The move follows other energy-driven aluminium curtailments and occurs amid falling premiums. | Soaring European power and gas prices have driven widespread aluminum smelter curtailments, including cuts at Neuss (Germany) and Dunkerque (France). Despite the supply reductions, aluminum prices are still falling to a 17-month low, with recession fears and rising Chinese output capping upside. Eurometaux warns the crisis also affects copper and nickel.
