news_summary: SMM reports aluminium prices surged on week amid continuing supply disruptions. Yunnan smelters are expected to cut output 10–20% after the Mid-Autumn Festival, while overseas cuts exceed 1.5 million tonnes this year. Social inventories are falling, and downstream restocking is improving, though demand remains softer. | SMM reports China’s molten aluminium casting billet operating rates fell to 67.84% in August, citing weaker downstream extruder orders, COVID disruptions, and power rationing. Higher silicon and magnesium costs raised billet costs, while some regions faced extended restart delays. September/October demand pessimism implies further operating-rate declines. | SMM reports China’s metallurgical-grade alumina prices mostly range-bound, but overseas deals rose due to frequent smelter output cuts. In China, some refineries face pandemic-related closed-off management, potentially tightening supply, while aluminium smelters have already started small-scale output cuts. Demand weakening may cap further gains. | Rio Tinto cut Rusal out of its Gladstone, Queensland alumina refinery operations to avoid breaching Australian sanctions on Russian oligarchs linked to Rusal. Rusal’s subsidiary sued, but the dispute underscores an immediate disruption to Russian-linked alumina bauxite processing and alumina export flows, which can tighten aluminium-related supply. | SMM says China’s aluminium ingot social inventories rose to 696,000 mt on Sep 13, up from the prior week, driven by holiday arrivals and slower pickup. Downstream buyers stayed cautious given high aluminium prices. Sichuan smelters are restarting slowly, while Yunnan cut fears eased, adding supply clarity. | Constellium said it will supply aluminium auto body sheet solutions for Mercedes-Benz C-Class vehicles produced in Europe, China, and South Africa, including hood, roof, tailgate and fenders, using plants in France and Germany. news_summary: After the US Fed’s 75 bp rate hike on Sept 21 (in line with expectations), aluminium prices rebounded sharply on Sept 22, with downstream buyers returning and stocking ahead of the National Day holiday. Traders cite support from possible production cuts, but note accepted Yunnan supply reductions and import pressure may weigh on prices.

news_summary: SMM says aluminium scrap throughput in Linyi (Sep 19–25) rose 20% WoW as retailers shipped in panic to mitigate falling prices. A00 aluminium stayed volatile and declined WoW; downstream scrap users bought less. Inventory fell, but oversupply of scrap is expected to persist this week, weighing on aluminium prices. Copper scrap throughput also increased. | SMM says a signing ceremony took place in Jiayuguan, Gansu for a 6 million mt/year alumina and materials project. The planned upstream alumina refining is intended to extend and integrate the local aluminium chain, covering alumina refining, aluminium smelting, semis and deep processing, potentially improving supply expectations for aluminium. | SMM’s summit takeaway argues China’s dual-carbon policies will accelerate low-carbon secondary aluminium growth faster than primary in the long run. It highlights expanding low-carbon supply in Yunnan into 2025, switching to green power, and carbon-related cost pressures (carbon trading and EU CBAM) that influence aluminium pricing. | Copper prices rose Tuesday as a softer U.S. dollar made the dollar-priced metal cheaper, but growth and demand concerns from aggressive global rate hikes limited upside. LME copper added 1.2%. The piece also notes small declines in LME aluminium and ShFE nickel amid generally mixed moves across metals inventories. | Element 29 reported an initial inferred mineral resource for its Elida porphyry copper-molybdenum deposit in Peru, including 321.7 million tonnes grading 0.32% copper. The update informs future drilling and potential project economics but does not indicate near-term production changes, limiting immediate price impact mainly to copper. | Norsk Hydro said it is responding to reduced aluminium demand by partially curtailing production. The move can tighten near-term aluminium supply, offering some support to prices. However, the decision is driven by softer demand, which may cap gains and keep price support moderate.

news_summary: ARENA will fund a Spinifex offshore wind farm resource assessment aimed at integrating variable renewable power with the Portland, Victoria aluminium smelter. The smelter can produce 358,000 tonnes per year and is a major power user. The project is positioned to support low-emissions aluminium production via steady electricity supply.

Reuters links the move to tighter monetary expectations. Aluminium was up modestly, indicating less consistent pressure across industrial metals. | Almost all aluminium premiums fell as discounting widened and demand weakened across regions. US, Rotterdam and other European premiums hit multi-month lows amid “freefall” discounting and liquidity competition. news_summary: Russian-origin copper has accumulated in LME warehouses in Germany, the Netherlands and Taiwan since mid-September, with stocks rising sharply. Some consumers reportedly reject Russian copper, turning LME storage into a last-resort outlet. LME also discussed possible bans on Russian aluminium, nickel and copper. | The article reports that the White House is weighing a ban on Russian aluminum in response to Ukraine-war escalation. Such a restriction could limit Russia’s access to export markets and reduce available supply, increasing expectations of tighter global aluminum availability and supporting prices in the near term. | The Biden administration is weighing an outright ban, punitive tariffs, or sanctions targeting Russian aluminum producer Rusal to respond to Moscow’s escalation in Ukraine. If implemented, reduced Russian supply would likely tighten global availability and boost aluminum prices in the coming days and weeks. | The article headline says aluminium prices are surging after the White House weighs a ban on Russian metal. Such a policy move would likely reduce near-term Russian aluminium availability and increase supply risk, supporting firmer spot pricing over the coming days. | Rusal says it will meet with the LME to oppose a possible metal ban. Any restriction would likely tighten supply and support higher aluminum prices near term. | Bloomberg reports the US is considering a complete ban on Russian aluminum, alongside potential tariff increases and sanctions on Rusal. While the US would receive less Russian metal, the article warns Russian supply could be redirected into global markets, potentially increasing dumping and suppressing aluminium prices. | The piece says Iran’s aluminum ingot production rose 23% in the first part of the year, indicating increased domestic output. Higher production can expand near-term aluminum supply and influence short-term market expectations for inventory tightness and pricing, especially if volumes affect regional export or availability.

news_summary: China exported 496,000 mt of unwrought aluminium and aluminium semis in September, down 8.22% month-on-month but slightly up 0.8% year-on-year. Cumulative exports in January–September rose 27.8% versus the prior period, according to customs data, implying stronger outward supply flows. | SMM reports SHFE and LME aluminium prices fell on the week, with SHFE at 18,535 yuan/mt and LME near $2,212.5/mt. Supply recovered slightly, but Yunnan power risks persist and COVID disruptions reduced arrivals and social inventories. Consumption was hit by COVID and downstream plant shutdowns, while hawkish Fed views weigh sentiment. | Alcoa is reportedly lobbying the Biden administration to curb Russian aluminium imports from Rusal, with options including a full ban, higher tariffs and sanctions. The piece notes that prior Rusal sanctions lifted LME aluminium and increased China aluminium exports. A new ban could tighten supply and support aluminium prices amid mixed demand outlook. | Rusal filed a London lawsuit alleging Norilsk Nickel CEO Vladimir Potanin breached a decade-old shareholder pact that was meant to protect Nornickel’s dividend formula. The dispute renews tension between Rusal and Potanin-controlled Interros, with the agreement set to expire and merger talks postponed. | China’s primary aluminium imports rose 31.13% month-on-month to 64,685.05 mt in September, though they fell year-on-year. Russia supplied the most, with imports of 45,666.12 mt, up 8.79% month-on-month and 447.23% year-on-year, indicating stronger near-term inbound supply. | China’s September bauxite imports fell to 8.21 million mt, down 23.57% month-on-month. Guinea remained the top supplier, with volumes from Guinea at 4.15 million mt, down 33.7% MoM. The decline suggests reduced upstream raw material inflows for China’s alumina/aluminium sector. | China’s primary aluminium output rose 9.3% y/y in September to 3.42 million tonnes after authorities relaxed power restrictions for smelters. Production was still down 2.5% month-on-month because Yunnan cut power from Sept. 10 due to tight hydropower. Overall supply trends point to bearish near-term price pressure for aluminium.

news_summary: A resurgence of COVID in Henan is disrupting secondary aluminium (remelted billets/ADC12) producers. Lockdowns and transport restrictions delay shipments, increase freight costs, and limit access to aluminium scrap/raw materials, narrowing margins and reducing production or output. Firms are generally bearish on near-term downstream order recovery. | CME Group and Marex will expand clients’ access to CME’s base metals markets, starting with CME COMEX Aluminum futures, offered on Marex’s Neon Metals platform. CME cites rising aluminum futures volumes and record open interest. The move is aimed at improving market access and risk management rather than changing fundamentals. | Johor Customs in Malaysia stopped a shipping company from exporting about 73,696kg of aluminium scrap in three containers. The shipment, valued at RM819,000, included export duties. The seizure removes a small but immediate amount of aluminium scrap from outgoing trade flows, which can modestly support prices. | Aldel’s aluminium smelter in Delfzijl has been granted creditor protection while seeking new financing after shutting down due to high energy prices and lack of government support. It previously stopped processing imported aluminium, and since then has remained in care-and-maintenance, prompting curators to find a buyer. | SMM reports aluminium scrap throughput in Linyi, Shandong fell 11.8% WoW to about 16,900 mt as pandemic controls disrupted logistics. Secondary producer operating rates rose only slightly and Linyi market was shut temporarily. Spot A00 aluminium prices dipped and SHFE aluminium (2211) weakened, indicating bearish near-term aluminium pressure. | SMM reports falling aluminium spot prices with low south China arrivals, firm spot premiums, and improving inventory only slightly after a recent LME surge. Demand is weak as PMI signals deteriorate and processing rates decline. Yunnan power shortage could reduce output; alumina prices fall, weakening cost support, while high energy costs keep aluminium rangebound.

news_summary: SMM reports China aluminium extrusion imports rose 12.4% YoY in Jan–Sep 2022, driven by higher inflows from Taiwan, while exports rose slightly 2.57% despite anti-dumping actions. Europe’s energy crisis and a low SHFE/LME ratio supported exports, but later rate hikes and weaker demand pressured shipments. | SMM said China’s aluminium ingot social inventories were 621,000 mt, slightly down WoW, but sluggish transactions and transport disruptions led to local accumulation, with more arrivals expected after Gongyi railway restores. Aluminium billet inventories fell WoW to 55,800 mt due to reduced arrivals from logistics issues and Yunnan smelter cuts, but SMM expects inventory growth once controls ease. | A study for the Beer Institute says Section 232 aluminum tariffs since March 2018 have cost the U.S. beverage industry $1.714 billion on 8.203 million metric tons of aluminum, largely charged through end-user pricing by mills and smelters. Brewers use aluminum heavily for cans, and the report urges tariff relief. | Research for the Beer Institute estimates U.S. beverage producers paid $1.714 billion in Section 232 aluminum tariffs between 2018 and August 2022. It argues rolling mills and smelters pass through tariff-burdened costs to brewers, raising production costs even when specific shipments are not tariffed.

news_summary: SMM reports China’s tightening aluminium capacity-approval regime has encouraged smelters to secure or trade quotas to commission new projects. It highlights Shanxi Zhongrun’s 65,000 mt/year capacity replacement project scheduled for December. Overall, 228,000 mt/year of aluminium capacity is expected to be commissioned in the year through replacements. | DutchNews.nl reports that the Aldel aluminium smelter in Delfzijl was declared bankrupt shortly after court protection. Operations had already been curtailed, citing high energy prices and insufficient government support. The closure leaves about 120 workers without jobs and previously stopped processing imported aluminium, reducing supply. | Citibank deposited bulk aluminium into LME warehouses in early October as higher interest rates reduced profitability of financing trades and some deals expired. LME aluminium stocks more than doubled, backwardation/premiums eased, and analysts cited slowing demand—together pointing to increased near-term supply and bearish sentiment for aluminium.

news_summary: SMM reports SHFE and LME aluminium fell on the week, with bearish sentiment driven by ECB/Fed tightening expectations, fears of global recession, COVID disruption to downstream demand and logistics, and weaker cost support from lower alumina and thermal coal. EU gas strength may boost smelter output, but overall downside risk persists. | SMM says the weighted alumina index fell to 2,792 yuan/mt, with most regions down on the week. Some losses and shutdowns capped declines, but import windows remain difficult and more capacity is set to release in Shandong, implying a north China surplus. Southwest output cuts could pressure further, keeping the market pessimistic. | SMM says alumina prices inched lower, and thermal coal also fell slightly, while smelter electricity prices were broadly stable. Lower feedstock costs reduced total aluminium smelting costs by 52 yuan/mt and narrowed profit declines, with A00 at 18,300 yuan/mt. Electricity remains the key cost driver, limiting longer-term easing. | Crisil Ratings expects Indian aluminium producers’ operating margins to fall by over 1,200 bps to 22–24% in 2022-23, down from 36% last fiscal. The deterioration is attributed to lower realizations and higher production costs, mainly power, which may weigh on supply conditions and sentiment for aluminium prices. | Kuusakoski is investing in a new Finnish copper centre to recycle copper- and aluminium-containing cables, radiators and transformers. The plant will crush and separate material and produce cleaner copper and aluminium fractions, claiming ~40 tons of copper per week capacity. Completion is targeted for 2023, enhancing recycled supply locally.

news_summary: EU sectors call for cautious implementation of the CBAM/ETS as part of “Fit for 55”. CBAM would charge importers for emissions certificates from 2026, initially for aluminium and other high-carbon sectors, while companies warn soaring carbon costs and energy prices have already driven unprecedented aluminium capacity idling.

Democrats and Republicans criticize the lack of detail and vetting, but the deal is unlikely to be stopped, supporting near-term aluminum production expectations. | Copper prices edged lower as bleak near-term manufacturing and metals demand outweighed hopes that China may ease COVID-19 controls next year. Global risk sentiment weakened ahead of a large U.S. rate hike, likely stifling activity. Tight supply and low inventories were cited as support, leaving overall bias mixed. | The report says Ghana’s sole aluminum smelter has shut down as a pay strike threatens safety. With one of the key local primary production sources offline, short-term aluminum supply availability may tighten, likely supporting near-term aluminium prices on expectations of reduced output and higher costs. | Mississippi lawmakers are reportedly considering incentives to encourage development of an aluminum plant. Production incentives typically strengthen the case for new or expanded capacity, supporting near-term expectations for aluminum demand from construction/start-up activity and potential supply outlook. | Steel Dynamics selected Columbus, Mississippi for a greenfield aluminum flat-rolled mill using recycled scrap, targeting 650,000 tonnes of output and product lines for packaging and automotive. The project is expected to start mid-2025, and is positioned as a decarbonized expansion to meet customer co-location needs and supply-chain advantages. | Mississippi lawmakers are seeking approval of nearly $247 million in state incentives for an aluminum plant tied to Steel Dynamics. The project is expected to create around 1,000 jobs by 2029. While company details are undisclosed, the policy push is likely supportive for aluminum investment sentiment. | Base metals prices rose on Tuesday after widespread rumors that China might change/ease its zero-Covid policy. Analysts said reopening hopes lifted China risk assets and triggered short covering; nickel additionally benefited from strong EV sales.

news_summary: Mississippi lawmakers finalized incentive funding to attract Steel Dynamics’ $2.5 billion aluminum mill complex in Lowndes County. The project is expected to create about 1,000 jobs in seven years and includes multiple facilities. Officials cite workforce training and potential co-located industries, boosting near-term confidence for aluminium-related demand. | SMM expects China’s aluminium extrusion output in 2022 to fall 3.86% to 20.11 million mt, mainly due to a sharp drop in construction aluminium extrusion from pandemic-related disruptions and a real estate downturn. China’s elevated COVID cases added pressure on industrial and construction activity. The report also notes lower tin and lead, with aluminium dipping and nickel edging up. | Balco (Vedanta) conducted successful trial runs using biodiesel at its aluminium smelter to preheat molten-metal ladles by reducing moisture before casting. The state says the project is a major job creator with $2.5 billion investment and includes roads, land help, and income tax rebates.

news_summary: Mississippi lawmakers approved funding to support site preparation for a planned $2.5 billion aluminum mill near Lowndes County, including $81 million upfront for development needs such as land, environmental/engineering work, and some road/bridge repairs. Developers expect ground to break in December, with operations targeted for 2026. | SHFE aluminium gained after the Fed’s hike was largely digested, while aluminium prices were supported by easing supply pressure—rumored production cuts at Henan following other provinces—and continued destocking. Social inventories fell sharply and spot premiums stayed elevated despite weaker downstream buying, keeping aluminium near range highs. | SMM estimates China’s aluminium plate/sheet and strip output in 2022 at 12.9 million mt (+2% YoY), with aluminium foil output at 4.75 million mt (+3%). news_summary: SMM reports aluminium prices softened in Sept-Oct but remain supported by weak supply and falling inventories. Overseas smelters cut output due to high natural gas costs, while Yunnan smelters reduced production for power shortages. With low social inventories around 500,000-600,000 mt in November, prices are expected to find support. | SMM reports China’s metallurgical-grade alumina output rose but supply surplus is expanding, while alumina prices remain weak and raw material costs increased. news_summary: European Aluminium argues that CBAM should not include indirect emissions until an adequate methodology is developed for aluminium’s indirect carbon costs. The assets included the Arconic SMZ plant producing fabricated aluminium (extrusions and flat-rolled). The move follows Ukraine-related restrictions and ongoing legal disputes, implying reduced Russia-linked aluminium supply. | Mississippi Governor Tate Reeves will sign legislation for Steel Dynamics’ $2.5 billion aluminium plant in Lowndes County. The announcement can improve near-term sentiment for aluminium demand, though timing and magnitude are uncertain.

news_summary: Universal Copper reports completion of 3D geological modeling at its Poplar copper project, identifying higher-grade copper-gold-molybdenum-silver targets and citing recent drill intercepts. news_summary: Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) bought Clean Energy Certificates covering 1.1 million MWh of solar electricity from EWEC’s grid supply tied to Noor Abu Dhabi to support production of CelestiAL solar aluminium. EGA said the certified solar power enables around 80,000 tonnes of output and supports decarbonization goals. | The headline reports that an aluminium smelter has signed up for solar power supply. If the agreement lowers electricity costs and stabilizes energy availability, it can improve smelter margins and raise expectations for near-term aluminium output economics, which may be supportive for aluminium prices over coming days. | SMM says China’s aluminium ingot social inventories fell to a record low of 518,000 mt on Nov 24, down sharply on the week, with fewer cargo arrivals and ongoing supply tightness supported by smelter output cuts and higher aluminium liquid production. Billet inventories rose to 63,000 mt amid regional arrivals.

news_summary: SK On will build a nickel MHP facility in Indonesia with EcoPro and GEM, aiming for 30,000 tons of high-purity nickel starting Q3 2024. MHP is used to produce nickel sulfate and EV battery precursors. The project also seeks oxidized nickel ore supply, potentially supportive for nickel. | European aluminium premiums (P1020A, Rotterdam) have fallen sharply from earlier record highs as consumer demand weakens and freight rates decline, reducing import appeal. Traders expect further premium drops as destocking continues and the arbitration window with Asia may close. With smelter capacity offline, supply could tighten later, but near-term pressure remains.

news_summary: SMM says China’s aluminium ingot social inventories fell to 516,000 mt on Nov 28, a new low, down 2,000 mt from last Thursday and 97,000 mt from a month ago. Inventory changes vary by region, while weekly warehouse shipments declined amid fewer arrivals and cautious downstream buying. | Global Energy Metals reported encouraging assay results from initial field sampling at its Nevada Lovelock project, including copper up to 26.3% and nickel up to 0.309%, plus cobalt. The update is exploration-focused with no production or confirmed reserves, so any short-term price effect is likely limited and sentiment-driven.

news_summary: Copper prices rose 1.1% on Tuesday as investors bet China’s COVID protests could force faster easing of curbs, boosting growth and metals demand expectations. Easier property developer financing also supported the metals-intensive sector. The yuan’s strength made dollar metals cheaper for Chinese buyers, lifting LME copper. Aluminium and nickel rose too.

news_summary: SMM reports China’s aluminium ingot social inventory at 500,000 mt on Dec 1, down 18,000 mt on the week, mainly due to fewer arrivals after some smelters shipped south where premiums were higher. Yet premiums fell on weak downstream consumption. Billet inventories rose slightly and are expected to grow. | Odisha Chief Minister inaugurated Vedanta Aluminium Park in Jharsuguda, a joint venture with IDCO. The park is meant to attract downstream manufacturers that can draw hot (molten) aluminium from Vedanta’s smelter to produce finished products. This can improve near-term sentiment for aluminium demand.

news_summary: China imported about 110,678 mt of primary aluminium in November 2022, up 64.1% month-on-month but down 51.7% year-on-year. Imports totaled 540,400 mt in January–November, down 66% year-on-year. The monthly jump suggests improved near-term supply flows into the domestic aluminium market. | SHFE aluminium fell again as macro sentiment faded and aluminium fundamentals weighed on prices. Social inventories in major markets rose, with more ingot arrivals and weaker off-season/pandemic demand. Spot discounts widened and power rationing in Guizhou was reported to expand further, renewing fears of additional production disruption. Prices are expected rangebound near term. | China announced higher export tariffs on primary aluminium from Jan 1, 2023, replacing provisional duties with a 30% export levy. news_summary: SMM reports that from August 2022, Chinese aluminium smelters started curtailing potlines due to power rationing. Sichuan nearly shut down in August then partially recovered; Yunnan output fell over 20% and is expected to remain reduced until May 2023. Guizhou smelters cut capacity by ~30% and may reduce further. | LME aluminium inventories fell again, reaching 450,300 mt as of Dec 30, the lowest in over two months, signaling tighter prompt supply. SHFE aluminium inventories rose weekly to 95,881 mt and hit a one-month high, though they remain close to a six-year low. Net effect is supportive for aluminium. | SMM reports SHFE aluminium down 2.22% to 18,270 yuan/mt and LME aluminium also weaker. Spot prices trend lower as inventories build for three straight weeks, with domestic social inventory rising and downstream plant operating rates falling due to seasonal order weakness and pre-holiday demand slowdown. | Copper fell to two-month lows as traders prioritized demand fears from slowing global growth and rising COVID cases in China, outweighing dollar weakness. Aluminium also dropped on sliding energy prices that raise smelting economics. Nickel plunged alongside the broader base-metals sell-off.

news_summary: SMM reports that LME-tracked A00 aluminium prices started weak, falling alongside declines in aluminium scrap prices. Lower scrap costs eased billet conversion margins, but demand is faltering as downstream firms close early for Chinese New Year and logistics slow. Conversion margins vary by region (e.g., south China 270–390 yuan/mt). | SMM said China’s aluminium ingot social inventories rose to 576,000 mt on Jan 5, after a sharp weekly increase, despite still-near historical lows. Aluminium billet inventories also climbed to 79,700 mt. Muted spot demand around the New Year and expected further stock builds point to bearish near-term price pressure. | Guizhou Power Grid imposed a third round of winter power rationing on local aluminium smelters due to severe heating demand and falling hydropower output. Five producers must cut combined consumption by 500,000 kW within five days. Prior rounds already curtailed output, cumulatively affecting ~940,000 mt and likely extending shutdowns into early January.

news_summary: SMM reports alumina prices rising: the domestic weighted index reached 2,945 yuan/mt (+12) and overseas prices climbed to $355/mt FOB Western Australia. Gains were attributed to a 30% Kwinana refinery output cut. Cold weather, transport limits, and natural gas shortages constrained roasting capacity. | SMM reports SHFE and LME aluminium rose over the week, attributing gains to a softer US dollar and growing expectations that China’s demand will recover after the CNY holiday. It also cites power rationing-induced capacity cuts in southwest China as supply support, though inventories remain elevated and the market surplus persists. | FICCI is calling for higher import duties on aluminium. The proposed tariff would likely reduce imports and constrain near-term aluminium availability, supporting domestic prices. The impact is expected within days to weeks as traders price in the likelihood of higher duties and related demand for local supply.

news_summary: The Sydney Morning Herald reports that Alcoa is fined $55 million and faces further scrutiny over alleged environmental breaches tied to its WA bauxite mining exemptions, including potential tighter mining approvals. It also notes South32’s WA alumina business faces proposed environmental protections, threatening operational viability and supply. | Multiple reports describe Alcoa being fined $55 million and facing further scrutiny over clearing and bauxite mining approvals in Western Australia, alongside concerns that stricter environmental protections could threaten operations. South32’s WA alumina business valuation is cut amid proposed protections, implying potential production disruption. | London copper rose 0.58% as hopes grew for China’s post-COVID reopening demand recovery and the dollar weakened. Trading stayed subdued due to Lunar New Year closure. The piece also flags potential copper supply risk from social unrest in Peru. LME aluminium fell 0.56% at the same time. | Iranian officials say the country is studying to expand annual alumina output to 500,000 tonnes with Chinese support, improving self-sufficiency and reducing costly imports. Alumina is converted into aluminium metal, and the Jajarm refinery is already operating, with plans for higher capacity. | Alcoa has scrapped plans to increase bauxite mining for export from WA’s Northern Jarrah Forest. The company will preserve bauxite output for its three West Australian alumina refineries, prioritizing value-add. WA regulators are reviewing future mine footprint options amid rising environmental scrutiny.

news_summary: LME three-month copper edged up 0.34% to $9,388 per tonne as the dollar weakened, with weaker Chinese demand during Lunar New Year keeping trade lacklustre. Investors await U.S. flash PMI and growth data for direction on Fed policy. Aluminium was also slightly higher at $2,638.50.

news_summary: Fastmarkets reports Brazilian aluminium import markets are set to shrink as domestic producers raise premiums and the government tightens trade rules (ending duty-free quotas and imposing countervailing duties on Chinese rolled aluminium from April 2023). news_summary: Australia’s aluminium producers are urging the government to recognize bauxite, alumina and aluminium as “critical minerals” tied to the clean-energy transition. The sector cites demand growth for solar and wind and EVs, and promotes Australia’s downstream value chain. The proposal could improve sentiment toward aluminium prices and investment. | Aluminium Bahrain (Alba) reported Q4 2022 and full-year results, with lower Q4 profit amid challenging conditions. It argues these materials are essential for infrastructure supporting Australia’s emissions-reduction targets, though it says no shortage exists currently. The policy push could influence investment expectations, but no immediate market shock is cited.

news_summary: China’s regulators issued energy consumption ceilings for aluminium and alumina production, effective Jan 1, 2024. The policy targets a highly energy-intensive industry, potentially constraining capacity utilisation as capacity nears limits under “dual carbon” goals. The article also cites strengthening demand after reopening, implying upward pressure on aluminium prices. | Alcoa says a majority of workers at its San Ciprián Spain aluminium smelter endorsed an improved restart plan starting in phases from January 2024. The update raises capital commitments (including a new anode plant), supported by long-term wind PPAs pending permitting. Alcoa plans full restart by late 2025 and minimum 75% capacity in 2025-2026.

news_summary: SMM reports SHFE 2303 aluminium fell 1.2% to 18,515 yuan/mt and LME aluminium dropped 2.07% to $2,455/mt. Yunnan production cuts have not yet started and expectations for output curbs have weakened. With mixed bearish and bullish factors, aluminium prices are expected to remain rangebound short term. | SMM says aluminium ingot social inventories in China’s eight major markets rose to 1.22 million mt by Feb 13, up from Feb 6 and last Thursday, and indicating a seasonal accumulation phase. Growth is slowing as lower prices and production recovery spur downstream buying, likely keeping further buildup gradual over coming weeks. | London copper edged up 0.2% but stayed near a one-week low as traders were cautious ahead of U.S. CPI. The dollar’s rise weighed, but China bank-loan data supported demand hopes. LME aluminium also rose 0.4%, while SHFE nickel fell 3.4% amid weaker sentiment. | SMM reports that aluminium smelters using captive power saw electricity costs fall as coal prices dropped, with weighted electricity price slipping to 0.44 yuan/kwh by Feb 10. Smelting full costs declined, but profits narrowed due to lower aluminium prices (A00 at 18,570 yuan/mt). Further coal declines could keep aluminium costs falling. | The piece cites mixed drivers for aluminium: bullish Chinese macro support and improving downstream demand, plus lower coal and anode costs, offset by bearish US dollar/risk appetite after strong non-farm payrolls and Fed rate-hike comments. Yunnan output cuts are not yet enforced, while potential Russian aluminium tariff increases raise supply worries. Prices likely rangebound. | Contact Energy’s CEO says majority owner Rio Tinto is ready to negotiate a long-term power contract for the Tiwai Point aluminium smelter near Bluff. The move follows earlier concern the power price was too expensive. As a result, expectations for aluminium production stability improve. | DADA Holdings acquired a majority stake in Niche Fusina, an Italian aluminum rolling facility near Venice with cast house and rolling capacity (about 75,000 tons/year rolling and 95,000 tons/year slab). The plant uses substantial recycled aluminum and sustainability initiatives, supplying multiple industries across Europe and beyond.

news_summary: China imported 10.15 million mt of bauxite in December 2022, down 13.9% MoM but up 16.3% YoY, with major sources including Guinea, Australia and Indonesia. Full-year imports reached a record 125.67 million mt (+16.9% YoY), suggesting steady bauxite supply for aluminium/alumina production. | SMM reports aluminium ingot social inventories rose sharply in January and continued increasing after the CNY holidays. By Feb 10 inventories climbed from 740,000 mt to 1.19 million mt, the highest versus the past three years, and is projected to exceed 1.3 million mt by end-February, delaying destocking. | SMM reports that in January Chinese refined aluminium output fell and operating capacity declined further, with potential short-term output cuts in Yunnan. However, downstream consumption weakened during the off-season and CNY, and overseas semis demand deteriorated, driving higher social inventories and weighing on SHFE aluminium, expected to stay low before rebounding in mid-February. | Reports say CBP enforcement under the UFLPA is now targeting aluminum, including issuing detention notices and investigating aluminum products (notably automotive and Chapter 76 items). With no de minimis allowance, compliance and supply-chain disruptions could raise costs and reduce near-term availability in the U.S.
